title: Curated EXTENSIBLE known-compromised watchlist (git-tracked OSV, loader-merged, append-on-new-advisory) — seed with current entries
description:
## Goal
Build the EXTENSIBLE MECHANISM for a curated, git-tracked known-compromised watchlist that deps-scan's
S8 checks — NOT a one-off TeamPCP snapshot. The deliverable is the **format + the loader-merge + a
documented, human-gated APPEND process**, so EVERY future compromise (not just today's) is a small dated
entry someone can add in minutes. Seed it with the current confirmed entries as the FIRST batch. (There
WILL be more — this is the durable home for them.)

## Why (re-scoped per review)
Hard-coding "the TeamPCP campaign" is too specific — compromises are continuous. The watchlist must be a
LIVING, append-only list with a clear "a new advisory dropped → add an entry" path. This ticket is the
manual-curated FOUNDATION; wh-5es generalizes it (extensions, digests, multi-surface) and AUTOMATES the
updates (the sec-kb-refresh threat-watch feed). The entries below are DATA/examples, not the point.

## Prerequisites
- DEPENDS ON wh-4k9 (S8 version-aware match) — else popular packages FP-flag every user.
- `load_malware_db` (malware_db.py:26) already parses OSV docs → `{name:set[versions]}`; OSV is the
  interop standard (GHSA / OSV.dev / OSSF all export it), so the curated file scales + interops + the
  future automated feed appends in the SAME shape.
- Reconcile with wh-5es (the full shared watchlist) — ONE watchlist, this is the package foundation; do
  not fork. Shares the Trivy entry with wh-562.

## Steps
1. Add a GENERICALLY-NAMED curated watchlist `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/known-compromised.osv.json`
   (or a small `known-compromised/` dir, one file per ecosystem) — OSV format; each entry dated + carries
   its advisory `source`/`url`. NOT named after any one campaign.
2. Loader merge: `load_malware_db` (or the `supply_chain.py` caller) reads this curated file IN ADDITION
   to the optional big OSSF snapshot (union the maps); never-raise/degrade preserved.
3. Document the APPEND PROCESS + an entry TEMPLATE in `reference/MALWARE-DB.md`: "when a supply-chain
   compromise is confirmed (GHSA/OSV/OSSF), add a dated OSV entry here via a human-gated draft-PR
   (ADR-012): name, ecosystem, exact bad versions, source URL, retrieved date." This is the durable
   "add the next one" path.
4. SEED the FIRST dated batch (2026-06, each sourced): litellm [1.82.7,1.82.8] (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76);
   telnyx [4.87.1,4.87.2]; @tanstack/* (the compromised set — cite the postmortem); trivy [0.69.4]
   (GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23). These are the format's first examples, not hard-coded logic.
5. Tests: the loader merges the curated file; a seeded bad version flags (no OSSF snapshot needed); a
   safe version does NOT (needs wh-4k9); **EXTENSIBILITY: a NEW fixture entry added to the file flags
   WITHOUT any code change** (proves it's data-driven, not campaign-coded).

## Files to Modify/Create
- NEW `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/known-compromised.osv.json` (or dir)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/malware_db.py` (merge) or the caller
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/MALWARE-DB.md` (the append process + template)
- tests

## Verification
- package tests green
- `litellm==1.82.7` in a lockfile, NO OSSF snapshot → S8 HIGH supply-chain finding
- a SAFE version → no flag (relies on wh-4k9)
- **extensibility test:** adding a new (name, version) fixture entry to the curated file flags it with NO code edit
- the curated file validates as OSV; ships in the vendor manifest (deps-scan is a consumer skill)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] a GENERICALLY-NAMED git-tracked OSV watchlist + loader merge (NOT campaign-specific)
- [ ] MALWARE-DB.md documents the human-gated APPEND process + an entry template
- [ ] seeded with the current confirmed entries, each dated + sourced (data, not logic)
- [ ] **adding a new entry needs NO code change** (extensibility test passes)
- [ ] reconciles with wh-5es (one watchlist; this is the package foundation) + shares the Trivy entry with wh-562
- [ ] safe versions don't flag (relies on wh-4k9); tests green

## Rollback
`git revert` — remove the curated file + the merge.

## Notes
Manual-curated FOUNDATION. wh-5es adds extensions/digests + AUTOMATES updates via the sec-kb-refresh
threat-watch (poll GHSA/OSV/OSSF → propose entries in this same OSV shape, human-gated). Choosing OSV now
means the automated feed later appends without a reformat.

## References
docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md + _supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md; malware_db.py:26,47;
OSV format (osv.dev); ADR-012 (human-gated KB/watchlist), ADR-019 (supply-chain class); wh-5es (general watchlist),
wh-562 (tool entries), wh-4k9 (prereq).

title: deps-scan S8: version-aware match (fix the name-only false-positive bomb)
description:
## Goal
Fix the S8 false-positive bomb. `signal_s8` (`supply_chain.py:744`) matches a dependency by NAME ONLY
(`if d["name"] in malware_db`), ignoring the version — so a watchlist entry for a *popular* compromised
package (LiteLLM, @tanstack/*) would flag EVERY user of that package, not just the bad versions. Make
S8 version-aware so it only flags the actually-installed bad version. (docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md RQ3; implements wh-5es RQ3.)

## Prerequisites
- `is_known_bad(name, version, db)` already exists (`malware_db.py:47`) and matches name+version (or the
  `"*"` wildcard) — S8 just doesn't call it.
- The normalized deps carry a `version`, but it may be the MANIFEST RANGE, not the lockfile-RESOLVED
  version (the adapters set `lockfile_present` but don't extract resolved versions — the research flag).

## Steps
1. `signal_s8`: replace the name-only check with `is_known_bad(d["name"], d.get("version",""), malware_db)`
   so a hit requires the version to be on the bad list — preserving the whole-package-bad wildcard `"*"`.
2. Extract the LOCKFILE-RESOLVED version in the adapters (npm package-lock/pnpm-lock; pypi
   poetry.lock/uv.lock/pinned requirements) onto the normalized dep. When only a manifest RANGE exists
   (no lockfile), only the wildcard `"*"` entry may flag — NEVER flag a specific-version entry on a range
   (that is the FP).
3. Keep S8's degrade (no db → `[]`) + the pure-function / no-dep / no-network contract.

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` (`signal_s8` + npm/pypi resolved-version extraction)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/`

## Verification
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` green
- popular pkg at a SAFE resolved version + a bad-version watchlist entry → S8 does NOT flag (FP fixed)
- same pkg at the BAD resolved version → S8 flags; a wildcard `"*"` entry still flags any version

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `signal_s8` calls `is_known_bad` with the resolved version; name-only match removed
- [ ] adapters extract the lockfile-resolved version; range-only never produces a specific-version FP
- [ ] tests pin: safe+bad-entry → no flag; bad-version → flag; wildcard → flag
- [ ] S8 still degrades (no db → `[]`); pure function, stdlib-only

## Rollback
`git revert` — additive change to the matching logic.

## Notes
PREREQ for safely seeding popular packages into the watchlist (the campaign-seed ticket). Implements
wh-5es RQ3; ecosystem-key disambiguation is the wh-5es schema work (separate).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md (RQ3); `supply_chain.py:744`, `malware_db.py:47`; wh-5es.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09: premise confirmed (signal_s8 name-only @ supply_chain.py:754). FIXES: (1) is_known_bad is NOT imported into supply_chain.py — add the import. (2) HARD PREREQ — the adapters (parse_npm/parse_pypi) emit deps with 'spec' (manifest RANGE), NO 'version' key, so d.get('version') reads a nonexistent key; FIRST extract the LOCKFILE-RESOLVED version (greenfield: package-lock/pnpm-lock/poetry.lock/uv.lock). (3) TWO existing tests pin the OLD name-only S8 behavior and WILL break under version-aware match — find + update them (TDD: encode the new intent). Baseline green (78 passed, serial).

title: Interim quarantine: demote TeamPCP-compromised Trivy in SCANNER_PREFERENCE (SCA→OSV/Grype, IaC→Checkov)
description:
## Goal

**Fail-safe off the TeamPCP-compromised Trivy NOW** — demote it in `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` so the SCA
capability leads with **OSV-Scanner / Grype / govulncheck** and IaC with **Checkov**, behind the
existing capability interface (ADR-015), degrading to the Read/Grep/Glob floor (ADR-003) if none are
present. Strengthen the registry caveat. This is **hours of work, gated only by `test_registry_lock.py`
— it must NOT wait on the research spike's 1–2 day timebox** (tech-lead decoupling call). The full,
permanent control (integrity gate + checksum-verify + watchlist) is the spike's job.

## Prerequisites

- The compromise is CONFIRMED: **CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23** (TeamPCP, 2026-03-19; malicious
  binary v0.69.4, images v0.69.5/.6, `trivy-action` tags force-pushed except v0.35.0).
- We are PARTIALLY protected already (`tool-registry.md:50-53` pins away from the bad versions; we run
  the binary **offline** `--skip-db-update`, `deps-scan/SKILL.md:44`) — but the caveat is **prose, not
  enforced**, so a poisoned binary on PATH would still be invoked. This ticket makes the fail-safe the
  default ordering.
- `detect_tools.py:110-119` `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` is the registry's **executable twin**;
  `_shared/scripts/tests/test_registry_lock.py:42` asserts doc↔code parity — both must move together.

## Steps

1. **`detect_tools.py` — reorder `SCANNER_PREFERENCE`:** demote `trivy` BELOW `osv-scanner`/`grype`/
   `govulncheck` for **sca**, and BELOW `checkov` for **iac** (Trivy is currently the *lead* IaC tool,
   `detect_tools.py:116`). Do NOT delete Trivy — demote it (it returns once the spike's integrity gate
   clears a safe pinned+verified version).
2. **`tool-registry.md` — match the new order** (keep the lock test green) and **strengthen the Trivy
   caveat:** cite CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, the safe set (binary v0.69.2/.3, v0.70.0+/
   v0.71.0; `trivy-action` v0.35.0), and "demoted pending the supply-chain integrity gate (wh-562)".
3. Keep everything else untouched (surgical, Policy 3) — only the SCA/IaC ordering + the Trivy caveat.

## Files to Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py` (`SCANNER_PREFERENCE` order)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/tool-registry.md` (matching order + Trivy caveat)

## Verification

- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_registry_lock.py -q` → green (doc↔code parity holds)
- `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` no longer lists `trivy` first for `sca` or `iac`; OSV/Grype/govulncheck lead SCA, Checkov leads IaC
- a detect run on a project with no scanners still degrades to the floor (ADR-003) — no error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` demotes Trivy below OSV/Grype/govulncheck (sca) and below Checkov (iac); Trivy not deleted
- [ ] `tool-registry.md` order matches; `test_registry_lock.py` green
- [ ] Trivy caveat cites CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 + the safe set + "demoted pending the integrity gate"
- [ ] No other tool/order changes (surgical); floor degradation still works
- [ ] Commit cites the CVE + ADR-006/ADR-015/ADR-003

## Rollback

Revert the reorder once the spike's integrity gate clears a safe, pinned-and-verified Trivy version (the
demotion is intentionally reversible — Trivy is not removed).

## Notes

- This is the INTERIM fail-safe; the permanent decision (version-gate vs checksum-verify vs keep-demoted)
  is the spike (wh-562) RQ2/RQ3/RQ5. Decoupled so a confirmed-compromise mitigation isn't blocked.

## References

CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 (Aqua upstream advisory); ADR-006 (pin-and-verify), ADR-015
(swappable capability), ADR-003 (degrade to floor); `detect_tools.py:110-119`,
`tool-registry.md:50-53`, `test_registry_lock.py:42`; the parent spike wh-562.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09 — code-state corrections: (1) SCA demotion is a NO-OP — Trivy is ALREADY last in detect_tools.py:113-114 sca list. (2) 'grype' is NOT in SCANNER_PREFERENCE (0 occ; prose-only in tool-registry.md:30) — drop it as a code anchor. (3) THE ONE real edit: detect_tools.py:116 iac list — move ('trivy','*') AFTER ('checkov','*'). (4) test_detect_tools.py:100-108 (category_tool['iac']=='trivy' under _which_only('trivy')) STAYS GREEN — no test edit. (5) lock test does NOT enforce ORDER (capability-presence + the 0.7[01] version regex only) — keep a v0.70/v0.71 token in the caveat; doc-order is Policy-11 parity, not lock-forced. (6) add CVE-2026-33634/GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 to the registry caveat.

title: Spike: Trivy/TeamPCP supply-chain compromise — verify, scope exposure, design integrity gate + threat-watch
description:
## Research Question

Trivy — which white-hacker RUNS as its SCA + IaC capability (`tool-registry.md:30,38`;
`detect_tools.py:110-119` `SCANNER_PREFERENCE`) — was compromised by threat actor **TeamPCP**
(CONFIRMED upstream, see Background). This is the literal failure mode **ADR-006** exists to prevent
("the agent that scans for supply-chain risk must not be a supply-chain victim"). VERIFY fully, SCOPE
our exposure, and DESIGN the supply-chain-integrity response. Each RQ → a decision:

- **RQ1 — VERIFY + scope the compromise, artifact-by-artifact, with UPSTREAM cites.** Per artifact —
  **binary** versions, **Docker image** tags, the network-fetched **vuln-DB** (`trivy-db`/`trivy-java-db`),
  the **`trivy-action`** + `setup-trivy` Actions, **CI** blast radius: exact malicious/safe versions,
  the **vector** (imposter-commit / tag force-push), **IoCs** (C2, payload hashes), the **upstream**
  advisory (GHSA / CVE / Aqua) as primary — not a third-party blog. → CONFIRMED/PARTIAL/NOT-CONFIRMED.
- **RQ2 — Our EXPOSURE given HOW we invoke Trivy + ADR-006 pinning + the existing caveat.** We run the
  **binary, offline**: `trivy fs … --skip-db-update --format json` (`deps-scan/SKILL.md:44`); the floor
  has **no** Trivy subprocess/docker (`supply_chain.py:23-41`), `normalize_deps.py` only parses Trivy
  JSON. Decide the sharp question: **does pinning a VERSION help when the vector is a force-pushed tag
  or the DB — not the binary version we pin?** Build a 5-row matrix (binary / image / vuln-DB /
  trivy-action / CI) × {we-touch-it?, ADR-006-version-pin-defends?, gap}. → conclusion: version-tag pin
  is defeated by force-push → require **commit-SHA / image-digest / binary checksum/signature verify**;
  and `--skip-db-update` does NOT stop a poisoned **local binary**, so binary checksum-verify-at-install
  is OUR load-bearing control. Net-exposure rating with rationale.
- **RQ3 — Interim quarantine POLICY (rationale only; the ACTION is decoupled).** Decide the policy +
  framing for failing-safe off a compromised tool: re-order `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` so SCA falls back to
  OSV-Scanner/Grype/govulncheck and IaC to Checkov, behind the capability interface (ADR-015),
  degrading to the floor (ADR-003). **NOTE: the immediate quarantine ACTION is a SEPARATE,
  do-it-now ticket (see Follow-ups) — it must NOT wait on this spike's timebox.** RQ3 here only records
  the *why* + the longer-term policy (version-gate vs quarantine vs checksum-verify).
- **RQ4 — The TeamPCP "widely-used tooling" pattern + which OTHER registry tools are at risk.** TeamPCP
  also hit Checkmarx **KICS** and **LiteLLM** (PyPI). Audit the registry on 4 integrity-risk attributes:
  (a) force-pushable Action tags, (b) mutable Docker tags, (c) install-script package (PyPI/npm),
  (d) live DB/feed fetch at run time. → a prioritized at-risk list.
- **RQ5 — A supply-chain-INTEGRITY gate + a tool-compromise threat-watch (its OWN ADR; REFERENCES
  wh-xn0, does NOT fold into it).** Every registry tool carries a **pinned commit-SHA / image-digest +
  a checksum/signature-verify method** (ADR-006) and is checked against a **known-compromised
  watchlist**. The watchlist EXTENDS the `malware_db.py` known-bad pattern (`malware_db.py:16-26`) but
  keys on **{tool: {bad_versions, bad_digests/SHAs}}** — digest, not just version (RQ2 shows version is
  insufficient). Then a `sec-kb-refresh` **threat-watch feed arm** polls tool-compromise advisories
  (GHSA on the registry tools' repos, CISA, OSSF) → human-gated draft-PR registry edits
  (`sec-kb-refresh/SKILL.md:34-42`, never auto-merge). **Integrity is a DISTINCT concern from wh-xn0's
  license/data-egress admissibility screen** (tool provenance vs input screening), so it lands as its
  OWN ADR that cross-references wh-xn0/ADR-024 — no ordering dependency on an unwritten ADR.
- **RQ6 — A dated KB entry for the TeamPCP technique (ADR-019 `supply-chain` class).** Draft
  `AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002` (the file holds `-001` slopsquatting) documenting imposter-commit /
  tag-force-push / CI-secret-pivot + technique-level detections — via the `sec-kb-refresh` draft-PR
  gate (schema-valid, dated, sourced, never auto-merge), OR a follow-up ticket to do so.

## Timebox

**Timebox:** **1–2 days** (RQ1 verification is largely done in the grounding pass; the bulk is RQ2
exposure-mapping + RQ5 gate design). Extend only with justification.

## Background / Context

**Priority P1 — a tool the agent RUNS is compromised** (live integrity risk to the reviewer itself).
The triggering claim was **VERIFIED CONFIRMED (high confidence)** during spike grounding:

- **What happened:** **2026-03-19**, TeamPCP compromised Trivy (aquasecurity) —
  **CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23**. Incomplete credential rotation after a late-Feb breach →
  compromised `aqua-bot` → **imposter-commit force-push to 76/77 `trivy-action` tags** (only `v0.35.0`
  clean) + all `setup-trivy` tags, plus malicious **binary v0.69.4** and **Docker images v0.69.5/.6**
  (re-published 2026-03-22). Payload = "TeamPCP Cloud stealer" (memory dump + SSH/cloud/K8s/Docker/Git
  secret harvest + AES/RSA exfil). Real impact: EU Commission AWS-key abuse, ~71 EU entities, ~91.7 GB.
- **Safe set (Aqua):** binary **v0.69.2/.3** + **v0.70.0+/v0.71.0**; `trivy-action` **v0.35.0**;
  `setup-trivy` **v0.2.6**. The **vuln-DB** updates were **suspended** during remediation (availability
  hit; not confirmed-poisoned).
- **Campaign CONFIRMED & spreading:** same actor hit **Checkmarx KICS** + **LiteLLM** (PyPI).
- **We are PARTIALLY protected already (two axes):** the registry caveat (`tool-registry.md:50-53`)
  already names the malicious versions + "avoid v0.69.4 + images v0.69.5-.6"; and we invoke the binary
  **offline** (`SKILL.md:44`), never the Action/image/live-DB vectors. **But** the caveat is PROSE not
  an enforced gate, and a user could still have a poisoned binary on PATH — so the protection is real
  but partial.

**Sources (upstream first):** GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 / CVE-2026-33634 / Aqua advisory; Microsoft Security;
Unit42; Wiz; Sysdig.

**Constraints to cite, not re-debate:** ADR-002 (Trivy CLI-first — we run the binary, not the
Action/MCP), ADR-003 (degrade to floor, never block), ADR-006 (pin-and-verify — the binding principle;
this spike SHARPENS its granularity to SHA/digest), ADR-015 (swappable capability — Trivy is an
illustrative default; the SCA/IaC capability survives one tool's compromise), ADR-019 (`supply-chain`
technique_class), wh-xn0 → ADR-024 (license + data-egress admissibility — integrity REFERENCES it),
the `sec-kb-refresh` outer-loop threat-feed polling.

## Investigation Approach

1. **RQ1** — confirm + record the upstream advisory; per-artifact malicious/safe table + IoCs; honest verdict.
2. **RQ2** — read the real invocation path; build the 5-row exposure matrix; conclude on pinning
   granularity (version → SHA/digest/checksum) and OUR load-bearing control (binary checksum-verify).
3. **RQ3** — record the quarantine policy/rationale (the ACTION is the decoupled ticket).
4. **RQ4** — score each registry tool on the 4 integrity-risk attributes.
5. **RQ5** — design the integrity gate (pinned SHA/digest + verify-method + not-on-watchlist), the
   watchlist data shape (extends `malware_db.py` with digests), and the `sec-kb-refresh` feed arm;
   keep the check deterministic (Policy 5). Land as its OWN ADR referencing wh-xn0.
6. **RQ6** — draft `AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002` (or a follow-up via the `sec-kb-refresh` draft-PR gate).

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
1. Treat the triggering claim as a CLAIM to verify — UPSTREAM advisory (aquasecurity GHSA / CVE / Aqua)
   primary; corroborate with ≥2 reputable threat-intel. State CONFIRMED/PARTIAL/NOT-CONFIRMED + confidence.
2. Use Context7 for CLI/flag lookups (Trivy, OSV-Scanner/Grype/Checkov fallbacks).
3. Map every claim to a source (URL+version, or repo file:line).
4. Map options to white-hacker principles (ADR-003/006/015, Policy 5 deterministic-check), not generic pros/cons.
5. Integrity gate = its OWN ADR (next available number AT WRITE TIME); reference wh-xn0/ADR-024, do not fold.
6. Follow-up tickets for every implementation; spikes produce artifacts, not production code.
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md` (verdict + exposure
      matrix + quarantine rationale + integrity-gate/threat-watch design + sources).
- [ ] **A DECISION appended to `docs/ARD.md` at the NEXT AVAILABLE ADR NUMBER AT WRITE TIME** (do NOT
      hard-code a number or reserve one — last written is ADR-023; assign sequentially when written).
      It cross-references ADR-006/024/003/015 and states: the verification verdict, our exposure, the
      quarantine policy, and the integrity-gate + threat-watch design.
- [ ] Draft KB entry `AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002` (or a follow-up to draft it via `sec-kb-refresh`).
- [ ] Follow-up implementation ticket(s): the integrity gate, the threat-watch feed, the KB entry.
      (The **interim quarantine is already a SEPARATE do-it-now ticket** — not gated by this spike.)

## Findings Template

Copy to `docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md`:

```markdown
# Research: Trivy / TeamPCP supply-chain compromise — verification, exposure, integrity response
**Date:** 2026-06-09  **Spike:** <id>  **Status:** Draft | Final
## Summary  (verdict + confidence · our exposure + why · quarantine decision · the integrity design)
## RQ1 Verification (per-artifact malicious/safe table + IoCs + upstream cite)
## RQ2 Exposure matrix (vector × we-touch × version-pin-defends × gap) + pinning-granularity conclusion
## RQ3 Quarantine policy/rationale (action = the decoupled ticket)
## RQ4 Registry at-risk audit (4 attributes per tool)
## RQ5 Integrity gate + threat-watch (own ADR; watchlist = {tool:{bad_versions,bad_digests}}; feed arm)
## RQ6 KB entry AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002
## Decision (the ADR number assigned at write time + why a standalone ADR referencing wh-xn0)
## References (GHSA/CVE/Aqua/MS/Unit42/Wiz/Sysdig + file:line anchors)
## Follow-up Tasks
```

## Create Follow-up Tickets

```bash
# (interim quarantine is already its own do-it-now ticket — created alongside this spike)
bd create "Integrity gate: pinned SHA/digest + checksum-verify + known-compromised tool watchlist (extends malware_db pattern with digests)"
bd create "sec-kb-refresh: poll tool-compromise advisories (GHSA on registry tools, CISA, OSSF) → human-gated registry integrity drafts"
bd create "KB: draft AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 (TeamPCP imposter-commit / tag-force-push / CI-secret-pivot) via sec-kb-refresh draft-PR"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1 verdict stated plainly (CONFIRMED/PARTIAL/NOT-CONFIRMED + confidence) with the UPSTREAM
      advisory cited (GHSA/CVE/Aqua), not a blog as primary.
- [ ] RQ2 exposure matrix populated; pinning-granularity conclusion explicit (version-tag pin defeated
      by force-push → SHA/digest/checksum); net exposure rated against the real offline-binary path.
- [ ] RQ3 quarantine policy/rationale recorded; the immediate ACTION confirmed decoupled to its own ticket.
- [ ] RQ4 at-risk list scored on the 4 integrity attributes.
- [ ] RQ5 integrity-gate design: own ADR referencing wh-xn0 (NOT folded); deterministic check (Policy 5);
      watchlist schema = {tool:{bad_versions, bad_digests/SHAs}}; `sec-kb-refresh` feed arm specified.
- [ ] RQ6 KB entry drafted or a follow-up filed (schema-valid, dated, sourced, never auto-merge).
- [ ] DECISION appended to `docs/ARD.md` at the **next available number at write time** (no hard-coded
      / reserved number); cross-refs ADR-006/024/003/015.
- [ ] No-go stated: compromise CONFIRMED, but the integrity-gate + threat-watch is built regardless (it
      generalizes the whole TeamPCP-style class).
- [ ] Report written; follow-up impl tickets created; timebox respected or extension justified.

## Notes / Open Questions

- **Pinning paradox (crux of RQ2):** ADR-006 pins VERSIONS, but the vector was a tag force-push (a
  mutable ref). Only commit-SHA + image-digest + binary checksum/signature are immutable. Does the new
  ADR mandate SHA-granularity for EVERY tool, or only the high-risk (Action/image-distributed) ones?
- **Offline ≠ immune:** `--skip-db-update` sidesteps the DB + Action vectors, but a poisoned LOCAL
  binary is unaffected — binary checksum-verify-at-install is the load-bearing control for OUR path.
- **Scope guard (Policy 3):** does NOT re-open ADR-002 (CLI-first) or ADR-015 (capability layer);
  it sharpens ADR-006's granularity + adds an integrity gate. Trivy is pinned-and-verified or
  interim-quarantined behind the existing SCA/IaC interface, never silently removed.

description: Add an OFFLINE, static heuristic 'supply-chain-malware floor' behind the existing SCA capability (ADR-015) to catch NOVEL malicious/typosquatted packages that CVE-based SCA (npm audit/OSV-by-CVE) structurally misses. title: EPIC: supply-chain-resilient tooling — a self-correcting capability lifecycle (admit→pin/verify→diversify→monitor→retire)
description:
## Goal
Govern white-hacker's capability TOOLS by a SELF-CORRECTING LIFECYCLE so tool COMPROMISE *and*
STALENESS are normal, gated, reversible events — not recurring fire drills. This is the outer
self-improving loop (ADR-001) applied to tooling, and it turns the reactive supply-chain spikes into one
coherent program. Strategy: docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md.

## Why
Trivy compromised (TeamPCP, CVE-2026-33634) + trivy-mcp unmaintained/stale-pinned → a point-swap
(Grype/Checkov/OSV/gitleaks) is a TREADMILL: those can also be compromised or go stale (gitleaks is
already feature-complete → "Betterleaks"). The durable fix is the lifecycle, not a better list.

## The lifecycle (5 stages → owning tickets)
1. **ADMIT** — license (MIT/Apache) + data-egress (local/no-telemetry) + **MAINTENANCE** + integrity gates → wh-xn0 (+ staleness gate: child A)
2. **PIN+VERIFY** — SHA/digest pin (not tags) + checksum/cosign verify; CLI-first, NO MCP layer → wh-562
3. **DIVERSIFY** — multi-vendor per capability + cross-check → wh-nvk (Trivy→Grype/Checkov/OSV/gitleaks) + diversity POLICY (child B)
4. **MONITOR** — compromise feed + **STALENESS** feed → human-gated registry swaps → wh-5es (+ wh-4k9/wh-k6l watchlist) + staleness signal (child A)
5. **RETIRE+REPLACE** — quarantine → select → record → retire (reusable) → wh-d5b (Trivy, the first run) + the reusable process (child B)

## New work (the gaps the strategy adds)
- **Child A — maintenance/staleness health:** a cadence/last-commit/EOL gate on ADMIT (extends wh-xn0) + a staleness signal in the MONITOR feed (extends wh-5es). Staleness ≠ compromise (the trivy-mcp lesson).
- **Child B — lifecycle codification:** the STRATEGY ADR (next-available #) + the reusable RETIRE→REPLACE runbook (Trivy = the worked example) + DIVERSITY-by-design as registry policy.

## Definition of Done
- Strategy ratified as an ADR (next-available number at write time).
- ADMIT gate includes maintenance; the MONITOR feed includes staleness.
- RETIRE→REPLACE is a documented, reusable runbook (Trivy worked through it).
- Diversity-by-design is a stated registry policy.
- The registry self-corrects via the outer loop (sec-kb-refresh), human-gated.

## Implementation map
wh-xn0 (ADMIT) · wh-562 (PIN+VERIFY + compromise-monitor) · wh-5es (MONITOR) · wh-4k9 + wh-k6l (watchlist machinery) · wh-nvk (DIVERSIFY/Trivy-replacement) · wh-d5b (RETIRE/quarantine) · child A (staleness) · child B (lifecycle+ADR+diversity).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md; ADR-001/002/003/006/015/019; the trivy / monitoring / replacement research docs.

title: Spike: replace Trivy with a diversified multi-vendor SCA+IaC set (Grype/Syft + Checkov + OSV-Scanner + gitleaks; maintained, MIT/Apache, CI-SHA-pinned)
description:
## Research Question

The user has **DECIDED to drop Trivy** (the `trivy-mcp` wrapper is unmaintained + pins a stale binary;
Trivy itself was TeamPCP-compromised — CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, see
`docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md`). This spike **picks the permanent replacement(s)**
behind the SCA + IaC + container-image + secrets + SBOM capabilities Trivy served, and **validates +
extends** the user's proposed diversified, multi-vendor set. It does NOT re-debate the drop-Trivy
decision — that is settled. Each RQ → a decision.

- **RQ1 — MAP the capabilities Trivy did for us.** `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` `sca`=`("trivy","*")`
  (`detect_tools.py:114`) + `iac` lead `("trivy","*")` (`:116`); registry SCA + IaC/container/CI lines
  (`tool-registry.md:30,38`) + the Trivy pin caveat (`:50-53`). Output = a capabilities-to-replace table
  (SCA multi-ecosystem · IaC/misconfig Dockerfile/k8s/Helm/Terraform/CloudFormation · container-image
  CVE · secrets · SBOM). → an authoritative "what must be covered" list.
- **RQ2 — VALIDATE the user's 5-tool set on 5 dimensions → a scorecard.** For Grype(+Syft), Checkov,
  kube-linter, gitleaks, OSV-Scanner, verify from UPSTREAM (LICENSE + release history, not a blog):
  (a) **License** MIT/Apache-2.0 ONLY (reject BSD/LGPL/GPL/AGPL); (b) **Maintenance/reliability** —
  cadence, last commit, maintainer health, **bus-factor** (the trivy-mcp staleness lesson is the headline
  criterion — be rigorously honest; FLAG gitleaks' single-maintainer footprint); (c) **Data-egress** —
  offline? telemetry default + disable (Checkov's Prisma/`--bc-api-key` — verify the offline default);
  (d) **CI** — official Action + plain binary, pinnable to a **commit-SHA not a mutable tag** (the Trivy
  force-push lesson), checksum/cosign/SLSA verify (ADR-006); (e) **Coverage parity** vs RQ1. → GO/EXTEND/REJECT per tool.
- **RQ3 — EXTEND: name any GAP + confirm the multi-vendor-diversity thesis.** Validate the thesis
  explicitly (a diversified multi-vendor set is a SECURITY UPGRADE — no single maintainer compromise
  takes out the pipeline; thin package-managed binaries, no vendored-old-MCP trap). State the
  **bus-factor caveat** (diversity raises the count of supply-chain surfaces → every tool must pass the
  same pin+verify gate). Gaps: GH-Actions hardening (actionlint/zizmor — MIT, already in the registry
  IaC line — keep); Dockerfile (hadolint = GPL reject → Checkov covers it); Terraform/CloudFormation
  (Checkov); a second-source for IaC (kube-linter k8s — optional). **EXCLUDE Checkmarx KICS** (same
  TeamPCP campaign). → final set + additions.
- **RQ4 — MCP vs CLI-first.** The trivy-mcp trap (unmaintained 3rd-party MCP pinning a stale binary)
  argues AGAINST any 3rd-party MCP dependency. CLI-first behind the capability interface (ADR-002 CLI-first/
  MCP-optional, ADR-015 capability layer, ADR-006 pin+verify). → each replacement = a pinned,
  checksum/sig-verified binary behind the capability port; NO MCP wrapper.
- **RQ5 — CI: per-tool Action pinned to commit-SHA + verify.** For each official Action: the **commit-SHA
  pin** (immutable; the `trivy-action` tags were force-pushed — only a SHA holds) + checksum/cosign/SLSA
  verify at install (ADR-006). For the binary path: pinned-version + checksum-verify-at-install (the
  "poisoned local binary" residual risk). → a per-tool CI pinning spec.
- **RQ6 — DECISION → impl.** Recommend the final set; specify: drop Trivy from `SCANNER_PREFERENCE`
  (`detect_tools.py:113-116`) + the `tool-registry.md` SCA/IaC lines; add the set with license/maintenance/
  data-egress columns; **handle the lock test** — `test_registry_lock.py:51` asserts the Trivy regex
  `r"0\.7[01]"`; it must be **removed/replaced** when the Trivy pin line is retired (else the lock goes
  red). Reconcile with the sibling spikes (Dependencies). → impl ticket(s).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1–2 days (research artifacts, not production code).

## Background / Context

- **Decision made:** drop Trivy. Driver = (1) `trivy-mcp` unmaintained + pins a stale binary (the "MCP
  trap"); (2) Trivy upstream TeamPCP-compromised (CVE-2026-33634; force-pushed `trivy-action` tags +
  malicious binary v0.69.4 / images v0.69.5–.6). Verification + our (LOW–MEDIUM, partial) exposure are
  FINAL in `docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md`.
- **User's proposed set (the hypothesis):** Grype(+Syft) [Anchore] container/OCI image CVE+SBOM ·
  Checkov [Palo Alto] IaC/k8s/Helm/Terraform/Dockerfile misconfig · kube-linter [Red Hat] k8s-only
  optional · gitleaks [independent] secrets · OSV-Scanner [Google] dependency-vuln cross-check.
- **User's thesis (validate — it is strong):** a diversified multi-vendor set is a SECURITY UPGRADE over
  one do-everything tool; no vendored-old-version MCP layer; each CI-native + specialized.
- **VALIDATION DIGEST (verified upstream 2026-06-09 — confirm + cite in the report):** Grype Apache-2.0
  v0.110.0 cosign-signed (GO) · Syft Apache-2.0 v1.45.1 (GO) · Checkov Apache-2.0 3.2.533 ~2-4d cadence,
  Gate-2 PASS offline w/o `--bc-api-key` (GO) · kube-linter Apache-2.0 v0.8.3 ~3mo (EXTEND, optional) ·
  gitleaks MIT v8.30.1 single-maintainer/feature-complete→Betterleaks (GO + bus-factor caveat) ·
  OSV-Scanner Apache-2.0 v2.3.8 (GO) · KICS REJECT (TeamPCP). trufflehog AGPL = Gate-1 fail (not a swap).
- **Constraining ADRs:** ADR-002 (CLI-first/MCP-optional, `ARD.md:28`) · ADR-003 (degrade, `:39`) ·
  ADR-006 (pin+verify, `:66`) · ADR-015 (swappable capability, `:149`) · ADR-019 (`:244`). **Last WRITTEN
  ADR = ADR-023** (`:281`).
- **Honesty constraints (user-stated, load-bearing):** maintenance/reliability + telemetry are the two
  decision drivers — report rigorously, incl. gitleaks' bus-factor + Checkov's Prisma-API posture.

## Investigation Approach

1. RQ1 — read `detect_tools.py:110-119`, `tool-registry.md:30,38,50-53`; build the capabilities-to-replace table.
2. RQ2 — verify each tool upstream (LICENSE/SPDX + GitHub API `published_at` + release notes); fill the scorecard.
3. RQ3 — gaps (Actions hardening / Dockerfile / Terraform / second-source IaC); confirm the diversity thesis + bus-factor caveat; reject KICS.
4. RQ4/RQ5 — CLI-first (no MCP) + per-tool commit-SHA pin + checksum/cosign verify.
5. RQ6 — impl spec (registry + `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` + the `r"0\.7[01]"` lock fix); create follow-up ticket(s); reconcile with the sibling spikes.

## Dependencies / reconcile

- **wh-d5b** (interim quarantine — demote Trivy now): land it first (hours, gated by the lock test); this
  spike makes the removal PERMANENT + names the replacement (supersedes wh-d5b's "returns when cleared").
- **wh-562** (integrity gate + checksum-verify + compromised-tool watchlist): the per-replacement
  pin+verify (RQ5) MUST plug into THAT one gate — do not invent a second mechanism.
- **wh-xn0** (admissibility: MIT/Apache-only license + local/no-telemetry data-egress gates): every
  replacement MUST pass wh-xn0's two gates; RQ2 (a)+(c) ARE those gates — cite, don't re-derive.

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report `docs/research/20260609_trivy_replacement_sca_iac.md` — the scorecard +
      coverage-parity matrix + the diversity-thesis verdict; every claim sourced to an upstream URL.
- [ ] An **ADR at the NEXT AVAILABLE number at write time** appended to `docs/ARD.md` (last written =
      ADR-023; 024/025 are CLAIMED by in-flight spikes wh-xn0 + the GDPR spike but NOT yet written — do
      NOT hard-code; take the next free number when you write). Decision = "drop Trivy; adopt the
      diversified split."
- [ ] Impl follow-up ticket(s): remove Trivy from `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` (`detect_tools.py:113-116`) + the
      `tool-registry.md` SCA/IaC lines; add the set with license/maintenance/data-egress columns;
      **remove/replace the `r"0\.7[01]"` assertion at `test_registry_lock.py:51`**; per-tool commit-SHA
      pin + checksum/cosign verify wired through wh-562's gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1–RQ6 each answered with a decision.
- [ ] Scorecard fully populated; every license/maintenance/telemetry claim cited to an UPSTREAM source.
- [ ] Coverage parity vs Trivy shown (no silent gap); KICS explicitly excluded (TeamPCP).
- [ ] Multi-vendor-diversity thesis validated WITH the bus-factor caveat (gitleaks single-maintainer).
- [ ] CLI-first / no-MCP confirmed (ADR-002/006/015); per-tool commit-SHA pin + verify specified (ADR-006).
- [ ] Report + ADR (next-available number) written; impl ticket(s) created; reconciled with wh-d5b/wh-562/wh-xn0.
- [ ] The `test_registry_lock.py:51` Trivy-regex handling is named in the impl spec.
- [ ] Timebox respected or extended with justification.

## Expected Outcome (valid result)

"Use the diversified split — **Grype (+Syft) + Checkov + OSV-Scanner + gitleaks**, with **kube-linter**
(k8s) + **actionlint/zizmor** (GH-Actions) as needed" is the expected, valid outcome. The spike grounds
it with verified upstream evidence + wires pin+verify per tool — it does not relitigate dropping Trivy.

## Notes / Open Questions

- gitleaks feature-complete (security patches only; author → "Betterleaks") — admit now (MIT, patched,
  mature domain), track Betterleaks via `sec-kb-refresh`. trufflehog (the obvious alt) is AGPL = Gate-1 fail.
- Checkov: run **offline, without `--bc-api-key`** to keep Gate-2 (no source upload / Prisma enrich).
  Confirm the exact offline invocation in the report.
- ADR race: three spikes target 024/025 — coordinate the final number AT WRITE TIME, don't pre-assign.

=> DO NOT symlink or regenerate one from the other. That would destroy the dogfood agent.

Real defects to fix:
1. NAME COLLISION (reachable): both are `name: white-hacker`. devops.md:154,174 dogfood via `claude --plugin-dir ./plugins/white-hacker`, which loads BOTH agents into one session under the same name -> one shadows the other / ambiguous subagent_type.
2. STALE CANONICAL DOC: .claude/CLAUDE.md "Architecture at a glance" says: "One agent .claude/agents/white-hacker.md (the senior-security-engineer identity + stage dispatch)". Wrong on both counts - the senior-security-engineer generic identity lives at plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md; the .claude/ file is the dogfood teammate; and there is not "one" agent.
design:
DECISION (GROOMED): NOT symlink, NOT regenerate. The two agents are intentionally distinct AND consistent with ADR-017 (.claude/ = dev scaffolding vs plugins/<name>/ = shipped payload, docs/ARD.md:199): the dev tree carries a TEAM of dev agents incl. a dogfood white-hacker; the payload ships ONE generic agent. Defects are narrow: (1) both got `name: white-hacker`; (2) ADR-009 + .claude/CLAUDE.md:57 were never updated after ADR-017 relocated the shipped identity to plugins/.

Reconciliation (3 parts):
1. APPEND a new ADR (next ADR-0NN; ADRs are append-only, Policy 11 - do NOT edit ADR-009/017). Record the two-agent reality: shipped generic = plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md (ADR-017 payload); dogfood dev teammate = .claude/agents/<new-name>.md (ADR-017 .claude/=dev). State it clarifies/supersedes ADR-009 ("identity once at .claude/agents/white-hacker.md" - path relocated by ADR-017, single-agent framing now two). Decide the de-collision here; cite ADR-004 (identity preservation, docs/ARD.md:47).
2. DE-COLLIDE the dogfood agent: rename .claude/agents/white-hacker.md `name:` (e.g. white-hacker-selfreview) [optionally the filename] so it does not shadow the shipped agent during the documented dogfood `claude --plugin-dir ./plugins/white-hacker` (ADR-017, docs/ARD.md:210). Update launch-team.md team table (line 78) + teammate refs (48,87,88,103,156,186) + devops.md + researcher.md.
3. SYNC derived docs: .claude/CLAUDE.md:57 "Architecture at a glance" -> describe BOTH agents at their correct paths (file is 91 lines now; keep <200, Policy 6).

OPEN (settle at claim, does NOT block grooming): confirm how Claude Code resolves a plugin agent vs a project agent sharing `name:` under --plugin-dir (1-command smoke in this repo, or ask claude-code-guide). If CC already namespaces them benignly, step 2 may reduce to a doc note; the ADR (1) + doc sync (3) still stand.

OUT OF SCOPE: wh-00i (edits ONLY the shipped agent Execution-budget section).
acceptance_criteria:
- No two simultaneously-loadable agents share `name: white-hacker` (verified by loading both via --plugin-dir), OR the collision is documented benign with the actual Claude Code resolution behavior cited.
- .claude/CLAUDE.md "Architecture at a glance" names BOTH agents at their correct paths (shipped generic vs dogfood teammate); no longer claims .claude/agents/white-hacker.md is the senior-security-engineer identity.
- launch-team.md / devops.md / researcher.md references to the dogfood agent are consistent with whatever name is chosen.
- .claude/CLAUDE.md stays < 200 lines.
- pytest + `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` stay green.
notes:
GROOMING REPORT wh-g2v (2026-06-09) - VERDICT: READY.
- Files real: plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md (15984B), .claude/agents/white-hacker.md (14567B), .claude/commands/launch-team.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md (91 lines, <200 OK). All exist.
- KEY GROOM FINDING: the "one agent" claim is anchored in ADR-009 (docs/ARD.md:105-110, accepted) - "Define the identity once (.claude/agents/white-hacker.md)". ADR-017 (docs/ARD.md:199-230, ".claude/ dev vs plugins/ payload") relocated the shipped identity to plugins/ AND documents the `--plugin-dir` dogfood (line 210) that makes the name collision reachable. ADR-004 (line 47) = identity preservation. => reconciliation needs an APPENDED ADR + de-collision + doc sync, NOT a one-line edit. Design updated accordingly.
- Active agent registry in this dev session resolves `white-hacker` to the DOGFOOD copy (tools Read/Grep/Glob/Bash/SendMessage, no ToolSearch) - confirms .claude/ project agent shadows in dev; the plugin copy is source-under-development, not installed.
- Scope: agents/ + docs/ARD.md + .claude/CLAUDE.md + launch-team.md (no skill package, no _shared, no schema, no finding-schema/kb_refs). Single coherent concern. No bd blockers. NOT wh-00i. Related: wh-3sl (stale skills/ dev-paths).

title: KB: AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 — TeamPCP imposter-commit / tag-force-push / CI-secret-pivot technique
description:
## Goal
Capture the TeamPCP technique as a dated, sourced KB entry **AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002** (under the ADR-019
`supply-chain` technique_class): compromising widely-used dev/security tooling supply chains via
imposter-commit / tag force-push / CI-secret pivot (Trivy, LiteLLM, Telnyx, @tanstack, Nx Console), so
the inner loop watches for it. (wh-562 RQ6 + the two research docs.)

## Prerequisites
- `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain.md` already holds AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-001 (slopsquatting);
  ADR-019 added the `supply-chain` technique_class.
- KB entries are human-gated draft-PR (ADR-012) + schema-valid (`validate_kb`): mandatory
  `metadata.source` (AML.T../ASI../CVE-..), `url`, `retrieved`.
- Verified facts + GHSA/CVE sources are in the two research docs.

## Steps
1. Draft AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 in `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain.md`: the technique
   (imposter-commit / tag-force-push / CI-secret pivot to weaponize widely-used tooling) + technique-level
   detections (pin to commit-SHA/digest NOT tags; checksum/signature verify; a known-compromised
   watchlist; "force-push defeats version-tag pinning"); cross-refs CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76 /
   GHSA-c9j4-9m59-847w + OWASP LLM03; dated + sourced.
2. Validate: `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts ... validate_kb.py` passes
   (schema, mandatory source/url/retrieved, size caps).
3. Route via the sec-kb-refresh draft-PR gate (never auto-merge, ADR-012).

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain.md` (append AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002)

## Verification
- `validate_kb` passes (schema-valid, source/url/retrieved present, size caps); unique dated id

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 drafted: technique + technique-level detections + dated GHSA/CVE sources
- [ ] `validate_kb` green; mandatory metadata present
- [ ] human-gated draft-PR, never auto-merged (ADR-012)

## Rollback
`git revert` the KB entry.

## Notes
Implements wh-562 RQ6. The detector CODE (watchlist/integrity gate) is separate (the seed ticket +
wh-562 RQ5) — the KB documents the TECHNIQUE (KB-as-knowledge / detector-as-code split, Rule 5).

## References
docs/research/20260609_*.md; ADR-019, ADR-012; `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain.md` (the -001 precedent); wh-562 (RQ6).
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09 — DEFECT (proved): do NOT append -002 to reference/supply-chain.md — FALSE-GREEN (validate_kb.split_front_matter stops at the FIRST closing fence; the appended entry becomes BODY, never schema/id/size-checked, yet validate_kb exits 0). Convention SKILL.md:27-28/:46 = ONE entry per file → CREATE A NEW FILE reference/supply-chain-2.md for AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002. Also: (a) validate cmd needs '--with pyyaml'; (b) Verification must assert the NEW id appears in validator output (no false-green); (c) xref: CVE-2026-33634 is Trivy's (GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, not in ticket) — pair each id with its product.

title: Spike: continuous supply-chain-compromise monitoring (target deps + IDE/VS Code extensions vs a shared watchlist; install + periodic)
description:
> **Scope:** This spike protects the **USER's project + machine**. It is the *sibling* of wh-562
> (which protects the agent's OWN tools, e.g. Trivy). The two SHARE one watchlist/feed mechanism —
> **do not build a second feed**. Reconcile, don't fork (ADR-015).
>
> **Verify-first status (CONFIRMED upstream at grooming, 2026-06-09 — keep, re-cite, don't re-debate):**
> LiteLLM PyPI v1.82.7/1.82.8 (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76); Telnyx PyPI v4.87.1/4.87.2; ~42 `@tanstack/*` npm
> pkgs / 84 versions (Mini Shai-Hulud, 2026-05-11); ~3,800 GitHub **internal** repos exfiltrated via
> the poisoned **Nx Console** VS Code extension nrwl.angular-console v18.95.0 (GHSA-c9j4-9m59-847w /
> CVE-2026-48027, OpenVSX also hit) — on top of the confirmed Trivy CVE-2026-33634.

## Research Question

The TeamPCP campaign proved one watchlist must cover **three surfaces at once**: poisoned **PyPI/npm
dependencies** (LiteLLM, TanStack), poisoned **IDE extensions** (Nx Console — a NEW surface white-hacker
does not scan today), and the resulting **repo/credential theft**. white-hacker already has an offline
known-bad-PACKAGE check (deps-scan S8 + `malware_db.py`) but: (a) it is only RUN on demand inside a
review, not at onboarding or periodically; (b) it does NOT cover the user's installed **IDE extensions**
at all; (c) there is no wired mechanism to keep the watchlist CURRENT from the outer-loop feeds. This
spike designs the **continuous** check. Each RQ → a decision.

- **RQ1 — VERIFY the campaign scope (DONE at grooming; record the verdicts + upstream cites).** Confirm
  artifact-by-artifact with PRIMARY advisories (GHSA/CVE/NVD), not blogs: LiteLLM, Telnyx, the
  `@tanstack/*` npm set, the ~3,800-repo breach, the Nx Console **extension**. → CONFIRMED/PARTIAL/
  UNCONFIRMED per claim. (Pre-verdict: all CONFIRMED; the only PARTIAL is *which feed mirror* carries
  each entry — RQ2. The report PRESENTS this, it is the evidentiary base.)
- **RQ2 — THREAT-INTEL INGESTION + the shared WATCHLIST data shape (the "how do we know" arm).** Decide
  the feeds + the on-disk watchlist schema that EXTENDS `malware_db.py`. Feeds (reuse `sec-kb-refresh`
  + `si-07-threat-feeds.md` — OSV.dev, GitHub Global Advisory DB, + OSSF `malicious-packages`
  [Apache-2.0, OSV], CISA KEV). THREE entry kinds behind one shape: `packages:{ecosystem,name,
  bad_versions[]|"*",bad_digests[]}` (extends the current `{name:set[versions]}` — add `ecosystem` to
  stop cross-ecosystem false-matches, optional `bad_digests` for the force-push case); `extensions:
  {marketplace,id,bad_versions[]}`; `repos:[...]` (low priority — likely DROP for a per-user tool).
  DECISIONS: (a) the exact JSON schema (OSV-superset so `load_malware_db` still parses the package kind
  unchanged); (b) **reconcile with wh-562** — agent-tool + target watchlists are ONE file/feed/updater
  with a `target:{dependency|tool|extension}` tag, NOT two; (c) human-gated draft-PR only (ADR-012,
  never auto-merge); (d) pin+verify each snapshot per ADR-006 (the wh-8qw pinned-OSSF-commit pattern).
- **RQ3 — TARGET DEPENDENCY CHECK: gap-analyze the EXISTING S8/malware_db coverage, then feed the
  current watchlist in (REUSE — minimal new code).** `signal_s8` (`supply_chain.py:744`) matches a dep
  NAME against the map; `is_known_bad` (`malware_db.py:47`) matches name+version (or `"*"`); the
  npm/PyPI adapters already parse the campaign's ecosystems. GAPS: (1) **S8 matches by NAME only** —
  LiteLLM/TanStack/Telnyx are bad at *specific* versions, so a name-only hit flags EVERY user of those
  popular packages (massive FP). DECISION: tighten S8 to `is_known_bad(name, resolved_version, db)`
  against the **lockfile-resolved** version (the adapters set `lockfile_present` but don't extract
  resolved versions — scope that). (2) **Ecosystem disambiguation** (PyPI `litellm` ≠ npm `litellm`).
  (3) **Digest match** (force-push defeats version-pin; compare resolved digests when present).
  → a SMALL additive diff to `signal_s8`+`malware_db.py` (pure functions, no new dep), NOT a rewrite +
  a coverage matrix {LiteLLM,Telnyx,TanStack,Nx-ext} × {today, after-fix, gap}.
- **RQ4 — NEW SURFACE: the IDE/VS Code EXTENSION scan (white-hacker cannot do this today).** Enumerate
  installed extensions + check vs the `extensions` watchlist. DECISIONS: (1) **Enumeration (degrade,
  ADR-003):** prefer `code --list-extensions --show-versions` (+ `cursor`/`codium`); fall back to the
  on-disk dirs (`~/.vscode/extensions/`, `~/.vscode-server/`, `~/.cursor/`, `~/.vscode-oss/`) parsing
  each `package.json`. No `code` + no dir → `tools_unavailable`, cap confidence, NEVER block. (2)
  **WHERE it lives:** a NEW `sec-extensions` skill behind a NEW `ide-hygiene` capability vs a function
  in deps-scan. LEAN: a thin new module behind a capability port (ADR-015) — extensions are a
  machine-level surface — but REUSE `malware_db.py`'s matcher (the signal is literally `is_known_bad`).
  (3) define the port `list_installed_extensions() -> [{marketplace,id,version}]`. (4) MACHINE scan → RQ7.
- **RQ5 — CADENCE: periodic WITHOUT a heavy scheduler (THE key open decision).** The user AVOIDS
  `/loop`/cron/`ScheduleWakeup` (project memory `avoid-schedulers-and-loops`) yet asks for "periodic."
  Weigh, honoring the resource caps (CLAUDE.md §Resource discipline, ADR-023 — no fan-out, bounded I/O):
  on-demand command · **fast SessionStart watchlist-DRIFT check** · install-time + documented re-run ·
  Claude Code scheduled task (REJECT — a scheduler) · CI job (OPT-IN extra) · git hook (REJECT default).
  RECOMMENDED: **on-demand command + a FAST SessionStart drift check, NOT a daemon** — bounded (read a
  pinned watchlist + the already-parsed lockfile + one `code --list-extensions`; no recursive walk),
  FACTUAL `additionalContext` (ADR-017 / `is_factual`, ≤10k, project scope), silent degrade. CI is opt-in.
- **RQ6 — INSTALL-TIME WIRING into sec-init / onboarding (wh-0vx).** `sec-init` writes the gated
  `.white-hacker/project-profile.json` (`additionalProperties:false`, `is_factual`, `<8000` bytes,
  Phase-9 gate). DECIDE how the first deps+extensions check wires in WITHOUT breaking those contracts:
  LEAN — record a FACTUAL summary line ("0 installed deps/extensions match the 2026-06 watchlist"), keep
  the watchlist DATA out of the committed profile (it lives pinned in `reference/`). Reconcile with
  wh-0vx's in-flight schema changes (don't collide on keys).
- **RQ7 — SCOPE / PRIVACY boundary (scanning extensions = scanning the MACHINE).** The dep check stays
  in the authorized target (the repo); the EXTENSION check reads HOME / runs `code` — crossing onto the
  developer's machine. DECISIONS (reconcile with wh-81y): (1) **Consent** — a one-time explicit opt-in
  before the first machine scan; (2) **Read-only + no-egress** — compare against an OFFLINE pinned
  watchlist, zero network, never upload the extension list (Agents Rule of Two); (3) **Data
  minimization (wh-81y)** — the installed-extension list IS personal/environmental data; never write it
  to findings/KB/report/profile — surface only the MATCHES, not the inventory; (4) confirm the
  asymmetry: PUBLIC threat-intel in, the user's data never out.

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1–2 days (research + ADR + the epic/ticket breakdown; no production code — spike artifacts only).

## Background / Context

- **Triggering intel (CONFIRMED upstream, 2026):** the TeamPCP / Mini Shai-Hulud campaign hit PyPI
  (LiteLLM, Telnyx), npm (`@tanstack/*` ×42 / 84 versions), the VS Code Marketplace + OpenVSX (Nx Console
  v18.95.0), and led to ~3,800 GitHub **internal** repos exfiltrated — on top of Trivy CVE-2026-33634.
  The **extension** vector is the surface white-hacker has no coverage for today.
- **What already exists (REUSE — file:line):** `malware_db.py:26` `load_malware_db` + `:47`
  `is_known_bad` (offline `{name:set[versions]}`, wildcards `"*"`, degrades); `supply_chain.py:744`
  `signal_s8` (matches by NAME only — RQ3 gap) + the npm/PyPI/Go/Cargo/gem/Maven adapters + `score()`;
  `deps-scan/SKILL.md:80-84` (S8 + the pinned OSSF snapshot, wh-8qw); `sec-kb-refresh/SKILL.md` +
  `docs/research/si-07-threat-feeds.md` (the outer-loop poller, draft-PR, never auto-merge); `sec-init/
  SKILL.md` (onboarding writes the gated companion).
- **What's blocked:** the new `ide-hygiene` capability; the shared-watchlist schema (with wh-562); the
  cadence mechanism (with the no-scheduler constraint); the install wiring (with wh-0vx); the
  machine-scan privacy boundary (with wh-81y).
- **Binding ADRs (cite, don't re-debate):** ADR-003 (degrade to floor), ADR-006 (pin+verify; force-push
  defeats version-tag pin → digest/SHA), ADR-012 (living KB/watchlist: dated, sourced, human-gated
  draft-PR), ADR-015 (capability layer; depend on a port), ADR-019 (`supply-chain` technique_class).
  **Last WRITTEN ADR is ADR-023; 024/025/026 are RESERVED-not-written by wh-xn0/wh-81y/wh-562 — use the
  NEXT AVAILABLE number at WRITE TIME, do not hard-code.**
- **Related open spikes (reconcile, don't duplicate):** wh-562 (agent-TOOL watchlist — SHARE the
  feed/watchlist/updater), wh-81y (GDPR/PII — the machine scan is the load case), wh-xn0 (tool
  admissibility), wh-0vx (sec-init onboarding — the install wiring host).
- **Constraints:** resource discipline (no fan-out, bounded I/O — ADR-023); scheduler-aversion.

## Investigation Approach

1. Record the RQ1 verdicts with primary GHSA/CVE/NVD cites (verify-first done — PRESENT it).
2. Reconcile the watchlist with wh-562 FIRST (one schema serves both targets); then specialize the
   target-dep + extension entry kinds.
3. Gap-analyze S8 (read `signal_s8`+`is_known_bad`+the adapters vs the exact (name,version) pairs);
   write the coverage matrix; scope the minimal additive fix.
4. Prototype the extension enumerator as a throwaway `poc-ext-scan/` IF load-bearing (one pure
   `list_installed_extensions()` + `is_known_bad` reuse + tests + the degrade path). Bounded, read-only.
5. Decide cadence (RQ5 matrix); default on-demand + fast SessionStart drift; reject any daemon.
6. Write the ADR + the epic/ticket breakdown; recommend a SPLIT if too broad.

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
1. PRIMARY sources only for the campaign claims (GHSA/CVE/NVD/vendor postmortem) — state CONFIRMED/
   PARTIAL/UNCONFIRMED per artifact with the URL.
2. Use Context7 for CLI/library doc lookups (e.g. `code --list-extensions`).
3. Web search scoped to 2026 for the campaign + extension-scan tooling.
4. REUSE before you invent — cite the real file:line (`malware_db.py:47`, `signal_s8` @ `supply_chain.py:744`).
5. Map every option to the binding ADRs (003/006/012/015/019) + the resource caps + the scheduler-aversion.
6. ONE watchlist — reconcile with wh-562; do not propose a second feed.
7. Privacy is load-bearing — the extension scan touches the machine; reconcile with wh-81y.
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md` (RQ1 verdict
      table with upstream cites; the design).
- [ ] An ADR appended to `docs/ARD.md` at the **NEXT AVAILABLE number at write time** (reconcile with
      wh-562/wh-xn0/wh-81y — the watchlist/feed is ONE mechanism; this ADR governs the target+machine
      side and POINTS at the shared schema, it does not fork it).
- [ ] A coverage matrix {LiteLLM, Telnyx, TanStack, Nx-ext} × {today, after-fix, gap}.
- [ ] The shared-watchlist JSON schema (package/extension/repo entry kinds), OSV-superset.
- [ ] (If load-bearing) `docs/research/poc-ext-scan/` — a pure `list_installed_extensions()` + degrade
      path + tests (`uv run pytest` passing); else a documented decision that no PoC was needed.
- [ ] An EPIC + follow-up ticket breakdown (below).

## Findings Template

Copy to `docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md`:

```markdown
# Research: Continuous supply-chain-compromise monitoring (target deps + IDE extensions)
**Date:** 2026-06-09  **Spike:** <id>  **Status:** Draft | Final
## Summary  (shared watchlist w/ wh-562; deps = small S8 fix; extensions = new capability; cadence = on-demand + fast SessionStart drift)
## RQ1 — Campaign verification (primary sources)
| Artifact | Verdict | Versions/IoC | Primary source |
| Trivy (anchor) | CONFIRMED | bin v0.69.4, img 0.69.5/.6, action 76/77 tags | GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 / CVE-2026-33634 |
| LiteLLM (PyPI) | CONFIRMED | 1.82.7, 1.82.8 | GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76 |
| Telnyx (PyPI) | CONFIRMED | 4.87.1, 4.87.2 | Akamai / ISC SANS |
| @tanstack/* (npm) | CONFIRMED | ~42 pkgs / 84 versions (2026-05-11) | Wiz / TanStack postmortem |
| ~3,800 repos (GitHub internal) | CONFIRMED (internal, not OSS) | via Nx Console ext | BleepingComputer / Nx postmortem |
| Nx Console (VS Code ext) | CONFIRMED | nrwl.angular-console v18.95.0 | GHSA-c9j4-9m59-847w / CVE-2026-48027 |
## RQ2 — Feeds + shared watchlist schema (reconciled with wh-562)
## RQ3 — Target dependency coverage gap + the minimal S8 fix (matrix)
## RQ4 — IDE/extension scan: capability, enumerator, where it lives
## RQ5 — Cadence decision (on-demand + fast SessionStart drift; no daemon)
## RQ6 — Install-time wiring into sec-init (wh-0vx)
## RQ7 — Machine-scan privacy boundary (consent / read-only / no-egress; wh-81y)
## Recommendation  (lightest sufficient design; or "too broad — split into N")
## References  (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76, GHSA-c9j4-9m59-847w/CVE-2026-48027, GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, TanStack/Wiz/Nx postmortems, OSSF malicious-packages)
```

## Create Follow-up Tickets

Recommended EPIC + children (file with `/design-ticket`; SPLIT if grooming agrees it's broad):

```bash
bd create "EPIC: Continuous supply-chain-compromise monitoring (target deps + IDE extensions)"
bd create "Shared known-compromised watchlist: schema + feed + human-gated updater (RECONCILE with wh-562)" --parent <epic>
bd create "deps-scan S8 campaign coverage: version/ecosystem/digest-aware match (minimal additive fix)" --parent <epic>
bd create "NEW ide-hygiene capability + sec-extensions scan (enumerate vs watchlist; degrade if code absent)" --parent <epic>
bd create "Cadence: /sec-supply-watch on-demand command + fast SessionStart drift check (no daemon)" --parent <epic>
bd create "sec-init install-time first deps+extensions check (factual companion line; reconcile wh-0vx)" --parent <epic>
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1 verdicts recorded with PRIMARY (GHSA/CVE/NVD/postmortem) cites.
- [ ] RQ2 watchlist schema = an OSV-superset `load_malware_db` still parses (package kind) + extension
      + repo kinds, **explicitly reconciled with wh-562 as ONE feed+watchlist+updater** (a `target` tag).
- [ ] RQ3 coverage matrix populated; the name-only-match FP gap named + the minimal additive
      (version/ecosystem/digest-aware) fix scoped, citing `signal_s8`/`is_known_bad`.
- [ ] RQ4 decides WHERE the extension scan lives + the capability port + the degrade path.
- [ ] RQ5 recommends the lightest sufficient cadence (on-demand + fast SessionStart drift, NOT a
      scheduler) honoring BOTH the scheduler-aversion and the resource caps.
- [ ] RQ6 wires the install-time check into sec-init without breaking its contracts; reconciled w/ wh-0vx.
- [ ] RQ7 states the machine-scan privacy boundary (consent/read-only/no-egress/no-retention),
      reconciled with wh-81y; the extension inventory is NEVER stored/transmitted (only matches).
- [ ] ADR appended at the NEXT AVAILABLE number at write time (NOT hard-coded), pointing at the shared mechanism.
- [ ] Follow-up tickets (or a justified "split into N") created.
- [ ] Timebox respected or extension justified.

## Notes / Open Questions

- **Biggest open decision = RQ5 cadence.** Default: on-demand + a FAST SessionStart drift check — NO
  daemon/cron/`/loop` (respect the standing aversion). The user may prefer CI-only (drop SessionStart) —
  a one-line change to the recommendation, worth deciding before execution.
- **One watchlist, not two.** Whoever of {wh-562, this} lands first defines the schema; the second
  EXTENDS it (tool / extension `target` kind), never forks the feed/updater. Ordering flagged.
- **`repos` kind likely OUT of scope** for a per-developer tool (drop or note-only; decide in RQ2).
- **Split candidate:** if the extension surface (RQ4) proves large, split it into its own spike/epic and
  keep this one to deps-watchlist + cadence. "Too broad — split into N" is a valid outcome.
- **Resource discipline:** the SessionStart drift check must be bounded — pinned watchlist + the
  already-parsed lockfile + one `code --list-extensions`; no recursive HOME walk, no fan-out.

title: Drop BSD from .claude/agents license allow-lists (MIT/Apache-2.0 only, ADR-023)
