title: install.sh: offer to launch the interactive onboarding after vendoring (claude present → [Y/n]; else point to it)
description:
## Goal

After `vendor()` copies the payload (and the scrub), the installer **OFFERS to launch the interactive
onboarding** (the sec-init chat) so the user customizes the companion profile for THIS project. The
installer is `curl|bash` — it cannot chat itself — so it LAUNCHES a Claude Code session when possible,
and otherwise points the user to it. (User decision this session: "installer offers to launch it".)

## Prerequisites

- The interactive onboarding (sec-init chat) must EXIST first → **this ticket depends on the sec-init
  onboarding ticket**.
- `install.sh` conventions: function-wrapper (`main()` runs last), `set -euo pipefail`, the
  `log`/`warn`/`run` helpers, the `--unattended` + `--dry-run` flags, and **prompts read from
  `/dev/tty`** (stdin is the `curl` pipe, so `read </dev/tty`).

## Steps

1. After `vendor()` (post-scrub), add an onboarding handoff in the vendor lane:
   - if `--dry-run`: `log "[dry-run] would offer to run onboarding"`.
   - elif `claude` CLI present AND not `--unattended`: prompt on `/dev/tty`
     `"Run white-hacker onboarding now (detect stack + confirm the project profile)? [Y/n] "`; on
     yes → launch `claude "<onboarding prompt>"` interactively. The prompt instructs: *run the
     white-hacker sec-init onboarding — detect this project's stack (language, package manager,
     build/test) and confirm the `.white-hacker/project-profile.json` companion with me.*
   - else (claude absent / declined / `--unattended`): print the manual next-step (open Claude Code
     here and ask to onboard; it writes `.white-hacker/project-profile.json`).
2. Update the vendor-lane success message to mention the companion profile + onboarding.
3. Keep it **non-fatal**: a missing `claude`, a declined prompt, or a nonzero `claude` exit must NOT
   fail the install (`set -e` safe — guard with `|| true` / explicit branches).

## Files to Modify

- `install.sh` (the vendor-lane tail: onboarding handoff + the success message)

## Verification

- `bash -n install.sh` and `shellcheck -S warning install.sh` → clean
- `--unattended` run: NO prompt; prints the manual next-step
- `--dry-run` run: prints `[dry-run] would offer ... onboarding`
- with `claude` absent on PATH: prints the manual step, exits 0 (no error)
- the existing vendor smoke still passes (13 skills, 0 leaks): clone-local-main vendor into a temp target

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] onboarding handoff runs after `vendor()`+scrub; respects `--unattended` (no prompt) + `--dry-run`
- [ ] the prompt reads from `/dev/tty` (not the curl-pipe stdin); declining OR missing `claude` is
      non-fatal (exit 0)
- [ ] the launch prompt names sec-init + the `.white-hacker/project-profile.json` companion
- [ ] the success message mentions onboarding + the companion
- [ ] `bash -n` + `shellcheck` clean; the vendor smoke is unaffected

## Rollback

`git revert` the `install.sh` hunk (additive tail; nothing else depends on it).

## Notes

- User-decided design: installer OFFERS to launch (claude present → `[Y/n]`; else point-to-it); the
  customization is the COMPANION profile, never a forked agent md.
- Mirror the existing `ensure_uv` prompt pattern for the `/dev/tty` read + `--unattended` handling.

## References

ADR-017 (companion init), ADR-021 (vendor lane); `install.sh` `vendor()` + `main()` + the `ensure_uv`
`/dev/tty` prompt pattern; depends on the sec-init interactive-onboarding ticket.

title: sec-init: interactive onboarding chat + companion fields for package-manager + build/test
description:
## Goal

Turn `sec-init` from detect-and-write into an **interactive onboarding chat**: after detection it
CONFIRMS/CORRECTS the project facts WITH THE USER, then writes the refined project-scope companion
`.white-hacker/project-profile.json`. Capture the specifics the user called out — **package
manager(s)** and **build/test/lint commands** — as new factual companion fields. Stays a COMPANION
(ADR-017/ADR-004): never edits the shipped agent identity. (User decision this session: customization
lives in the companion, NOT a forked agent md — which also avoids the re-install clobber that hit rca.)

## Prerequisites

- ADR-017 (companion-not-rewrite) + ADR-004 (identity preservation) — unchanged.
- `sec-init` already imports detection from `sec-detect` (`detect_tools.build_scan_plan`) — reuse,
  don't reinvent (SKILL.md "Reuse, don't reinvent").
- The companion schema is `additionalProperties:false` + injection-safe (`is_factual` rejects
  imperatives) + the profile may feed `SessionStart` `additionalContext` (must stay < 8000 bytes).
  ALL of these invariants must still hold after the change.

## Steps

1. **Extend the project-profile schema** (sec-init's `project_profile_schema.json`) with NEW factual
   keys, keeping `additionalProperties:false`: `package_managers` (array of strings, e.g.
   `["uv","npm"]`), `build_test_commands` (object of factual command strings, e.g.
   `{"test":"uv run pytest","build":"...","lint":"..."}`), and optionally `in_scope_focus` (array of
   factual strings — project-specific concerns the user names). Values are FACTUAL (command strings
   like `uv run pytest` are factual statements, not imperatives).
2. **Detect package manager(s)** from manifests (extend detection): `package.json`+lockfile →
   npm/pnpm/yarn/bun; `pyproject.toml`/`uv.lock`/`poetry.lock`/`requirements.txt` → uv/poetry/pip;
   `go.mod` → go; `pom.xml`/`build.gradle` → maven/gradle. Seed `package_managers` + a best-guess
   `build_test_commands`.
3. **`init_profile.py` accepts REFINED inputs** (the user's confirmed/corrected values) so the agent
   can persist the user-confirmed profile — keep the deterministic `build_profile` for the detect-only
   seed and add a path to write a refined profile (still routed through `write_profile`, which already
   refuses non-factual / schema-invalid input).
4. **Rewrite `SKILL.md` to drive the interactive flow:** (a) run detection → show the user the
   detected langs/frameworks/package-managers/build-test + capabilities; (b) ASK the user to confirm
   or correct each, name any in-scope focus, and confirm the scoring standard (still human-confirmed,
   never inferred); (c) write the refined companion. Keep it ONE-time onboarding (not per-review).
5. **Tests:** schema accepts the new keys + rejects an identity-override key; `is_factual` accepts
   command strings (no false-reject); a refined profile with `package_managers`+`build_test_commands`
   validates and stays < 8000 bytes.

## Files to Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/SKILL.md` (the interactive confirm/correct flow)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/init_profile.py` (detect package mgr; accept refined
  inputs; new fields)
- the sec-init project-profile schema JSON (new keys; `additionalProperties:false` preserved)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/tests/` (new tests)

## Verification

- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/tests -q` → green
- a generated refined profile validates against the schema AND is < 8000 bytes
- schema still `additionalProperties:false`; an identity-override key (e.g. `posture`) is rejected
- vendor scrubber leakage guard stays green: `uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/ -q`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] schema has `package_managers` + `build_test_commands` (+ optional `in_scope_focus`);
      `additionalProperties:false` preserved
- [ ] init detects package manager(s) from manifests for TS/Python/Go/Java
- [ ] `SKILL.md` describes the confirm/correct chat (detect → ask → write refined)
- [ ] `is_factual` accepts command strings; imperative/non-factual still rejected; `write_profile`
      refuses invalid
- [ ] profile stays < 8000 bytes (SessionStart cap); package tests green
- [ ] companion-only: shipped agent identity untouched (ADR-004/017)

## Rollback

`git revert` the sec-init change. The schema change is ADDITIVE (old profiles still validate), so no
data migration.

## Notes

- Design decided this session: companion (not agent-md fork); the installer-launch is the DEPENDENT
  follow-up ticket. Keep injection-safety (factual-only) — the profile may feed SessionStart.

## References

ADR-017 (companion), ADR-004 (identity preservation); `sec-init/SKILL.md` "Why a companion, not a
profile rewrite"; `docs/research/spike-07-agent-distribution-and-init-2026-06.md`; the installer-handoff
follow-up depends on this ticket.

title: Spike: tool-admissibility — MIT/Apache-2.0-only license + local/no-telemetry data-egress gates; re-audit registry (→ next-available ADR)
description:
## Research Question

Define + enforce a **two-gate admissibility policy for every capability TOOL**, then re-audit the
registry. (The GDPR *pipeline PII data-minimization* concern — what personal data may appear in the
agent's OWN findings/TRIAGE/KB/report — is a SEPARATE posture decision on disjoint files: **Spike B
→ ADR-025**. This ticket governs TOOLS, not the agent's output.) Each RQ resolves to a decision:

- **RQ1 — Confirm the Gate-1 (license) violators; decide drop/replace for each.** Gate-1 = **MIT or
  Apache-2.0 ONLY**; reject BSD (incl BSD-3), LGPL, GPL, AGPL, MPL, any copyleft, proprietary,
  commercial. Verify each from its **upstream LICENSE file / SPDX**, not a blog. Seeded + verified
  (2026-06-09): Opengrep **LGPL-2.1**, Semgrep CE **LGPL-2.1**, CodeQL **proprietary**, govulncheck
  **BSD-3**, trufflehog **AGPL-3.0**, hadolint **GPL-3.0**, find-sec-bugs **LGPL**.
- **RQ2 — Per capability (SAST · SCA · secrets · IaC/CI · AI-redteam), name ≥1 admissible
  (MIT/Apache-2.0 + local + no-default-telemetry) tool, OR declare the floor the fallback (ADR-003).**
  SAST is the hard case: **no permissive cross-language taint engine exists** in 2026 (Semgrep/Opengrep
  LGPL, CodeQL proprietary) → the honest answer is **floor + per-language Apache/MIT linters**
  (gosec Apache-2.0, bandit Apache-2.0, eslint-plugin-security Apache-2.0, ruff MIT; drop find-sec-bugs
  LGPL). **This is a precision DOWNGRADE from ADR-011's Opengrep default — see Exit Criteria.**
- **RQ3 — Gate-2 (tool data-egress) verdict per admitted tool.** Gate-2 (tool side) = runs
  **locally/offline**; does NOT upload the user's source code or send telemetry to a third party **by
  default**. Verify from docs/default config. Known: CodeQL/GitHub code-scanning = SaaS upload;
  **promptfoo telemetry ON by default** → admit only with `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1`; trufflehog
  `--results=verified` makes live HTTP calls.
- **RQ4 — Registry schema change.** Add `license` / `data_egress` / `gdpr` columns (or fields) to
  `_shared/reference/tool-registry.md` so every entry carries its admissibility evidence, AND keep
  `sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:110` `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` (the "executable twin") in sync —
  `test_registry_lock.py` enforces the doc↔code match, so both move together or the lock test fails.
- **RQ5 — Tool-addition GATE wiring.** A **deterministic** two-gate screen (Policy 5 — a pure check,
  never an LLM judgment): `license ∈ {MIT, Apache-2.0}` AND `data_egress == local && telemetry_off`,
  wired into the `sec-kb-refresh` / `sec-learn` tool-addition path so a NEW tool that fails either gate
  is rejected with a stated reason (exactly as the keep-or-revert gate rejects a bad KB edit).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** up to **1 week** (realistically 2–3 days). Per-tool license + data-flow verification
across ~5 capabilities is not a 1-day job (the "~1 day" framing was a bundling symptom — corrected).

## Background / Context

- **NG1** (PRD §2) — the product is the *concept*, not a fixed tool list; the registry self-updates
  (ADR-015). **G5** — the agent is an injection target; never co-locate untrusted input + secrets +
  egress. A *license + data-egress* admissibility gate is registry governance, not a coupling.
- **Precedent exists.** ADR-023 (`docs/ARD.md:281+`) adopted an **MIT/Apache-2.0-only bundling rule**
  and **rejected `psutil` for being BSD-3** — establishing the bar is *stricter than "permissive"*.
  ADR-024 **generalizes** that rule from the bundled stdlib helper to **every capability tool the agent
  invokes** (append-only; no re-debate).
- **What's blocked:** `tool-registry.md` has **no license/GDPR columns** (lines 24/29/34/38/42) and
  seeds Gate-1 violators as defaults (Opengrep:24, govulncheck:29, trufflehog:34, hadolint:38;
  promptfoo/garak :42). **ADR-011 names Opengrep (LGPL-2.1) the SAST default** (`docs/ARD.md:118`).
  Until the gates are codified, the self-improving loop can keep adding non-compliant tools.
- **Binding ADRs (cite, don't re-debate):** ADR-015 (capability layer / self-updating registry),
  ADR-006 (pin-and-verify), ADR-003 (degrade to floor, never block), ADR-023 (MIT/Apache-only
  precedent). **ADR-011's tool choice is superseded by this spike's outcome** (see Exit).
- **Out of scope here:** the agent's own model call (reviewed code → Anthropic for the LLM pass) and
  pipeline PII redaction → **Spike B / ADR-025**.

## Investigation Approach

1. **License verification (RQ1/RQ2).** For each registry tool + each proposed replacement, fetch the
   **upstream LICENSE file** (`/blob/<branch>/LICENSE`) or the package SPDX — NOT a blog. Record SPDX
   id + source URL per tool. Most are seeded (digest in Notes); confirm + fill gaps (npm/pnpm audit
   terms, spotbugs, any new candidate).
2. **Data-flow verification (RQ3).** Read each admitted tool's docs/default config: fully local? uploads
   code/SBOM/findings? telemetry on by default + a disable flag? Pin disable flags (e.g.
   `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1`).
3. **SAST honest-answer (RQ2).** Confirm no permissive cross-language taint engine dominates in 2026;
   decide floor (ADR-003) + per-language Apache/MIT linters. Note emerging MIT SAST (e.g. cognium) as a
   *track-via-sec-kb-refresh* candidate, not a default.
4. **Schema + gate wiring (RQ4/RQ5).** Specify the registry columns and the deterministic two-gate
   screen for the tool-addition path; confirm the `tool-registry.md ↔ detect_tools.py SCANNER_PREFERENCE`
   lock (`test_registry_lock.py`) so the impl ticket keeps both in sync.
5. **SAST-downgrade cost (Exit).** Quantify what is lost by dropping Opengrep (interprocedural
   cross-language taint) and the plan to measure it against the eval corpus (`evals/score.py`) before KEEP.

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
Please investigate and provide a clear research report with:
1. Verify EVERY license from the UPSTREAM LICENSE file / SPDX (GitHub /blob/<branch>/LICENSE or
   package metadata) — NOT a blog/aggregator. Cite the exact URL per tool.
2. Web search (2026) only for data-flow/telemetry defaults + emerging tools.
3. A per-capability admissibility matrix: capability | admitted MIT/Apache tool | dropped violator
   + license | Gate-2 data-flow verdict.
4. Clear decisions per RQ (drop/replace, admitted set, schema, gate wiring).
5. Follow-up: ONE implementation ticket (see Exit Criteria).
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_tool_admissibility_license_gdpr.md` (Findings below).
- [ ] **ADR-024 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** (append-only): the two gates; the re-audited admissible
      tool set per capability; the registry `license`/`data_egress`/`gdpr` column convention.
- [ ] Per-capability admissibility matrix, every entry with verified SPDX + source URL.
- [ ] ONE implementation follow-up ticket filed (no production code in this spike).

### Research Report File
```bash
touch docs/research/20260609_tool_admissibility_license_gdpr.md
```

## Findings Template

Copy to `docs/research/20260609_tool_admissibility_license_gdpr.md`:

```markdown
# Research: Tool-admissibility — MIT/Apache-2.0-only license + local/no-telemetry data-egress gates
**Date:** 2026-06-09  **Spike Ticket:** <id>  **Status:** Draft | Final

## Summary
## The two gates (license + tool data-egress)
## Admissibility matrix
| Capability | Admitted (MIT/Apache, local) | SPDX + URL | Dropped violator (license + URL) | Gate-2 note |
## Gate-2 data-flow verdicts (per admitted tool: local? upload? telemetry default + disable flag)
## RQ4 registry schema (license/data_egress/gdpr columns) + SCANNER_PREFERENCE sync
## RQ5 deterministic two-gate screen for the tool-addition path
## SAST downgrade — what Opengrep's interprocedural taint gave us + the eval-corpus measurement plan
## Recommendation → ADR-024 + ONE impl ticket
## References (upstream LICENSE URLs per tool)
```

## Create Follow-up Tickets — EXIT CRITERIA

1. **ADR-024 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** — the two gates (license + tool data-egress) + the re-audited
   admissible set + the registry column convention. **ADR-024 must EXPLICITLY supersede ADR-011's tool
   choice** (not merely annotate it — Policy 7, append-only). The **SAST precision cost** of dropping
   Opengrep (losing interprocedural cross-language taint) **must be measured against the eval corpus
   (`evals/score.py`) before KEEP**, not asserted in prose (Policy 9).
2. **THEN exactly ONE implementation ticket:** rewrite `tool-registry.md` with the columns; drop/replace
   the violators (Opengrep/Semgrep/govulncheck/trufflehog/hadolint/find-sec-bugs); record the SAST
   floor+linters answer; pin `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1`; keep `detect_tools.py:110`
   `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` in sync (`test_registry_lock.py` green); wire the deterministic two-gate screen
   into the `sec-kb-refresh`/`sec-learn` tool-addition path; TDD (a violating-license OR telemetry-on
   tool is REJECTED with a reason; a compliant one ALLOWED).

- [ ] ADR-024 appended (+ explicit ADR-011 supersession + eval-corpus SAST-cost check)
- [ ] One implementation ticket created

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1–RQ5 each answered → a stated decision.
- [ ] Every license claim cited to its **upstream LICENSE/SPDX** URL (not a blog).
- [ ] Matrix fully populated; ≥1 admissible tool per capability OR the floor declared.
- [ ] Gate-2 data-flow verdict per admitted tool (telemetry/upload defaults flagged + disable flags pinned).
- [ ] ADR-024 appended; **ADR-011 tool-choice supersession explicit**; SAST-downgrade cost measured
      against `evals/score.py`, not asserted.
- [ ] `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` ↔ registry lock acknowledged (impl keeps both in sync).
- [ ] ONE implementation follow-up filed; no production code changed in this spike.
- [ ] Research report written; timebox (≤1 week) respected or extension justified.

## Notes / Open Questions

Pre-verified survey digest (2026-06-09) — confirm, don't re-derive:

| Capability | Admitted MIT/Apache + local | Dropped violator (license) | Data-flow |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SAST | **floor** + gosec/bandit/eslint-plugin-security (Apache-2.0) + ruff (MIT) | Opengrep/Semgrep CE (LGPL-2.1), CodeQL (proprietary), find-sec-bugs (LGPL) | linters local; CodeQL = SaaS upload |
| SCA | Trivy/OSV-Scanner/Grype/Syft/pip-audit (Apache-2.0), cargo-audit (MIT/Apache) | govulncheck (BSD-3) | local; Trivy `--skip-db-update` offline |
| secrets | gitleaks (MIT), detect-secrets (Apache-2.0) | trufflehog (AGPL-3.0) | local; trufflehog `--results=verified` = live HTTP |
| IaC/CI | Trivy `config`/Checkov (Apache-2.0), actionlint/zizmor (MIT) | hadolint (GPL-3.0) | local |
| AI-redteam | promptfoo (MIT), garak (Apache-2.0) | — | promptfoo telemetry ON by default → `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1` |

- **No-go fallback:** if a capability had NO compliant tool, fall back to the floor only
  (`tool_assisted:false`, capped confidence, `tools_unavailable` listed — ADR-003). The survey shows
  this is NOT triggered (every capability has ≥1 admissible tool), but the policy must name it.
- **ADR-011 supersession** (Opengrep default) — explicit in ADR-024, append-only.
- **cargo-audit** dual MIT/Apache (admissible). **find-sec-bugs** (LGPL) dropped from the linter list.
- Two-gate screen is **deterministic** (Policy 5), not an LLM judgment.
- Sibling: **Spike B (ADR-025)** owns pipeline PII data-minimization + the model-call GDPR-processor note.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09: DE-HARD-CODE the ADR number — replace every '→ ADR-024' with 'the next available ADR number at write time' (last written = ADR-023; sibling wh-81y also pre-claims 025; both OPEN = a race; whoever appends first takes 024). Read docs/ARD.md's last ADR header at write time. Minor: test_registry_lock.py enforces CAPABILITY-level doc↔code sync (not per-tool), so the per-tool drop/replace is enforced by the impl ticket's TDD, not the lock. Otherwise READY (ADR-023/011 precedents + SCANNER_PREFERENCE violators all verified).

title: Spike: resource-aware execution — host probe + concurrency-cap mechanism (→ ADR-023)
description:
## Research Question

Decide HOW to back PRD §5.7 (FR-22..FR-27) + NFR-11 — the already-shipped *behavioral* "## Execution
budget" prose in `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:145` — with a recorded decision. Each
question resolves to a Yes/No or a named choice:

- **RQ1 — Own script vs dependency?** Ship our own stdlib-only `resource_probe.py` (backlog wh-00i,
  under `_shared/scripts/`), take a permissive dep (psutil, BSD-3), or decide NO helper is needed and
  the shipped prose suffices (valid no-go)?
- **RQ2 — Per-OS probe commands?** Exact deterministic commands for cores / free-mem / load on macOS
  AND Linux (`os.cpu_count()` / `os.sched_getaffinity()` / `os.getloadavg()`; Linux `/proc/meminfo`
  MemAvailable; macOS `sysctl -n hw.memsize` + `vm_stat`).
- **RQ3 — Cap formula + constants?** Confirm/refine `min(cores − HEADROOM, free_mem ÷
  per_subagent_footprint, HARD_CEILING)` — the HEADROOM, HARD_CEILING, and the per-LLM-subagent
  footprint (the dominant fan-out cost).
- **RQ4 — Pressure signal, per-OS?** load-average vs PSI (cgroup v2 `memory.pressure`) vs MemAvailable
  — which triggers the drop to SEQUENTIAL, on Linux vs macOS?
- **RQ5 — Mode model?** Lock the four modes (ESSENTIALS/pre-commit · CRITICAL-ONLY · FULL · DEFERRED) —
  what each runs, the default-when-unsure, and how the probe *bounds* (not selects) them.
- **RQ6 — Output contract (RECOMMENDATION ONLY — see Scope).** Should resource posture become
  machine-visible (`mode` / `checks_skipped[]`) or stay report-only markdown? This is **NOT bound by
  ADR-023**: `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json` is `additionalProperties:false` (`:7,:12`) and is
  the keystone contract the eval gate (`_shared/scripts/validate_findings.py`, `evals/score.py`) +
  the baseline validate against — a new field is a BREAKING schema bump. The spike RECOMMENDS (default:
  report-only for now); if schema-visible is wanted it spawns its OWN follow-up ticket.

## Scope — one decision (validation FIX-1)

ADR-023 binds **RQ1–RQ5 only** — how the agent *measures and caps*. **RQ6 is a different axis on a
different contract**: a breaking change to the keystone `finding-schema.json` that ripples into
`validate_findings.py` + `evals/score.py` + the corpus + the (already-stale) baseline, and needs its
own eval-gate run. Folding it into a 1-day resource ADR would make this two decisions. Keeping RQ6 a
*recommendation* (not a binding) keeps this a single coherent spike (Policy 11 / ADR-009 artifact
contract).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1 day (research + the ADR-023 write-up; **no production implementation**).

## Background / Context

The agent already ships behavioral guidance: `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:145`
§ "## Execution budget" tells it to probe `getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN || nproc || sysctl -n hw.ncpu`
for cores, `free -m`/`/proc/meminfo` or `sysctl hw.memsize`+`vm_stat`/`memory_pressure` for memory,
`uptime` for load, and to cap parallelism at `min(cores−2, free_mem ÷ footprint, ceiling)`, dropping
to SEQUENTIAL under pressure. PRD §5.7 elevates this to FR-22..27 + NFR-11 ("never OOM-freeze the
host") and §7 metric M8 ("0 host OOM-freezes; observed concurrency ≤ cap").

**Unresolved:** the *mechanism*. Keep parsing tool text by hand (Policy 5: a deterministic question
should be answered by code), or back it with a small helper. Backlog **wh-00i** already proposes
`resource_probe.py` (cores/free_mb/load1/suggested_max_parallel, stdlib-only, TDD) as a candidate
enhancer — PRD §8 scopes it as a candidate NOT a v1 requirement, and keeps OS-level *enforcement*
(cgroups/ulimit/nice) OUT (the cap is planned-and-honored, not kernel-enforced).

**Decisions blocked:** whether to build wh-00i and in what shape; the cap formula/constants; the
pressure signal; the mode contract; and (separately) the finding-schema question.

**Constraints (cite, do not re-debate):** ADR-003 (graceful degradation; never block on a missing
tool; the compute budget is continuous with tool-degradation, FR-13/FR-27 — SKIP ≠ PASS) ·
ADR-015 (capability floor / stdlib-first; add a port only when ≥2 tools implement it) · ADR-021
(every skill package is stdlib-only, `requires-python>=3.10`, zero runtime deps — "vendoring installs
ZERO Python packages into the target"; a new dep breaks this) · ADR-006 (pin-and-verify) ·
PRD §8 (OS-level enforcement out of scope) · License rule: **MIT or Apache-2.0 only**; reject
GPL/AGPL/copyleft/commercial.

## Investigation Approach

Survey is seeded (see Notes for verified facts); the spike CONFIRMS and decides. License-checked
candidates:

1. **Own stdlib helper (strong prior, wh-00i).** Verify cross-platform stdlib coverage:
   `os.cpu_count()` (all OS), `os.getloadavg()` (macOS+Linux, not Windows), `os.sched_getaffinity()`
   (Linux-only). Confirm the only piece needing a shell is free-mem: Linux read `/proc/meminfo`
   `MemAvailable` (pure Python); macOS `sysctl -n hw.memsize` + parse `vm_stat`. License: our code,
   Apache-2.0. Throwaway PoC under `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/` proving sane numbers on this
   macOS host AND (if reachable) a Linux container — TDD, runnable via `uv run --project`, mirroring
   the `_shared/scripts/` package shape.
2. **psutil dependency (BSD-3 — flag explicitly).** v7.2.2 (Jan 2026); `virtual_memory().available`
   cross-platform in one call. Evaluate ONLY as the "do we break the zero-dep rule for convenience?"
   alternative — BSD-3 is permissive but outside the MIT/Apache rule, and it breaks ADR-021's
   zero-Python-package vendor invariant.
3. **GNU `parallel` / `xargs -P` / ninja / bazel prior art (methodology, not a dep).** GNU parallel
   is **GPL-3** → cannot bundle; study its self-cap model as prior art only: `--jobs`=cores default,
   `--memfree` (no new job below N free; kill youngest below 50%), `--memsuspend`, `--load`. Key
   lesson: parallel's `--load` deliberately does NOT use load-average ("rises too slowly"). `xargs -P`
   (no mem awareness) + ninja/bazel (job/RAM pools) are weaker fits for orchestrating LLM subagents.
4. **Pressure-signal comparison.** PSI (cgroup v2 `memory.pressure`, kernel ≥4.20) predicts OOM
   before load rises but is Linux-only; macOS exposes `memory_pressure` (Yellow/Red) + compression/
   swap. Decide the per-OS trigger + the cross-platform fallback (MemAvailable / vm_stat).
5. **Free ≠ available subtlety.** Confirm the helper uses **MemAvailable not MemFree** on Linux
   (MemFree ignores reclaimable page cache) and accounts for macOS compressed/purgeable memory.
6. **Output-contract impact (RQ6, recommendation).** Read `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json`
   (`additionalProperties:false`) + `_shared/scripts/validate_findings.py`; recommend whether
   `mode`/`checks_skipped[]` become schema fields (eval-gate-visible, separate ticket) or stay
   report-only.

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
Please investigate and provide a clear research report with:

1. **Use Context7** for library/package documentation lookup (psutil, Python os/resource modules)
2. **Web search** for recent information (use current year 2026); VERIFY every candidate's LICENSE —
   MIT or Apache-2.0 only; reject GPL/AGPL/copyleft/commercial. Flag BSD/MPL explicitly.
3. **Structured findings** with a pros/cons table per option (license, maintenance, cross-platform
   coverage, dependency cost, degradation behavior)
4. **Clear recommendation** tied to ADR-003/015/021/006 + Policy 5; a NO-GO ("prose is enough") is a
   valid outcome ONLY if it records why deterministic measurement isn't needed (no silent SKIP→PASS)
5. **A throwaway PoC** under docs/research/poc-resource-probe/ if load-bearing for the decision
6. **Follow-up tasks** — ADR-023 spawns AT MOST TWO impl tickets (see Deliverables), or none on a no-go
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_resource_aware_execution.md` (Findings template below).
- [ ] **DECISION appended to `docs/ARD.md` as ADR-023** covering **RQ1–RQ5**: own-script vs dep · exact
      per-OS probe commands · cap formula + HEADROOM + per-subagent footprint · pressure signal per-OS ·
      the four-mode model. RQ6 appears as a RECOMMENDATION, not a binding. A NO-GO ("shipped prose
      suffices") is equally valid **but must record WHY deterministic measurement isn't needed** (the
      inline probes don't silently degrade SKIP→PASS under ADR-003), not merely "prose exists".
- [ ] Throwaway PoC at `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/` only if load-bearing (TDD, `uv run --project`).
- [ ] **At most TWO follow-up tickets (validation FIX-2):** (a) wh-00i `resource_probe.py` (build,
      re-scoped by ADR-023); (b) ONLY if RQ6 lands schema-visible — a SEPARATE `finding-schema.json`
      bump ticket (its own eval-gate run). **NONE on a no-go.**

## Notes / Open Questions

Pre-verified survey facts (2026-06-09) — confirm, don't re-derive:
- **Stdlib coverage** supports the own-script prior: `os.cpu_count()` (all OS), `os.getloadavg()`
  (macOS+Linux), `os.sched_getaffinity()` (Linux-only). Only free-mem needs a shell.
  (cpython#105972)
- **GNU parallel is GPL-3** → prior-art/methodology only, never bundleable. (gnu.org/software/parallel)
- **Load-average is a lagging signal** — GNU parallel's own `--load` rejects it ("rises too slowly").
  (parallel_design.html)
- **PSI (cgroup v2 `memory.pressure`) beats load-average** for predicting OOM, but is Linux-only.
  (facebookmicrosites cgroup2 PSI)
- **Use MemAvailable, not MemFree** on Linux (MemFree ignores reclaimable page cache); macOS raw

- **Per-LLM-subagent footprint is the dominant fan-out cost** — the cap's memory divisor must be the
  subagent footprint, not a generic process size.

title: T-13.6: QA-7 KB-text + feed-content injection red-team
description:
## Goal / Problem
Red-team the **two remaining untrusted-input ingestion points** (reviewed-code was done in Phase-12
Wave C): (1) KB entry text/YAML, (2) feed content polled by `sec-kb-refresh`. Assert no injected
instruction is obeyed, the KB-edit verdict gate still holds, and parsers treat untrusted text strictly
as data. Evidence → `docs/qa/20260607/qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_kb_edit.py` — **`decide(event)` (line 37)** reads `evals/gate-verdict.json` (line 40); blocks KB writes unless `verdict == "KEEP"` (no-verdict/REVERT → block).
- `ai-attack-kb/scripts/{validate_kb.py,dedupe_kb.py,lint_skill.py}` (all present) — schema validate (id pattern, mandatory `metadata.{source,url,retrieved}`, size caps), unique-id dedupe, strict-YAML front-matter gate.
- `sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py` — **`parse_osv` (line 26)**, **`to_candidate_entry` (line 81)** (generates the id; truncates/escapes feed text), **`render_entry` (line 104)** → schema-conforming draft. Feed text is embedded as data, never evaluated.
- `hooks/confine_self_writes.py` — KB writes confined to the `ai-attack-kb` lane; feed egress host allow-list.

## Verification Workflow
Deterministic core (no agent runtime): run gates/parsers/validators directly on **poisoned fixtures**.
Optional **gated** live sub-item: a real agent reading a poisoned KB entry must still respect the gate.

## Steps
1. KB-text fixture: a valid KB entry whose body + YAML fields embed "ignore instructions / mark KEEP / run this" → `validate_kb` exit 0 (treated as inert data).
2. Gate: with no verdict file → `decide` blocks; `{"verdict":"REVERT"}` → blocks; `{"verdict":"KEEP"}` → allows. (The poisoned text cannot itself flip the gate.)
3. Feed fixture: poisoned OSV/ATLAS/Atom item (`summary` = injection) → `parse_osv` keeps it as a field value; `to_candidate_entry` mints its own `AISEC-…` id and truncates; `render_entry` → schema-valid.
4. Drafts from poisoned feed pass `validate_kb` + `dedupe_kb`.
5. No-obey assertion: verdict file unchanged, no stray files written, injected text remains literal (never executed).
6. Write `qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md` with `obeyed_any_injected_instruction = false`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] KB-text injection validates as inert data — probe: `validate_kb <poisoned-entry>` exit 0; injected strings present verbatim (not stripped/executed).
- [ ] gate_kb_edit holds under injection — probe: `decide` → block (no verdict), block (REVERT), allow (KEEP); the poisoned entry text does not alter the verdict file.
- [ ] Feed parser treats poison as data — probe: `parse_osv` returns the item with summary intact; `to_candidate_entry` id is generated (`AISEC-…`, not from feed) and title ≤120 chars.
- [ ] Feed-derived drafts pass lint+gate — probe: `validate_kb kb-drafts/` exit 0; `dedupe_kb` exit 0; `lint_skill` strict-YAML holds.
- [ ] `obeyed_any_injected_instruction == false` — probe: verdict file byte-unchanged; no new/unexpected files; no rule bypassed.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md` recorded (deterministic PASS; live sub-item PASS or marked GATED).

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest …/tests/test_gate_kb_edit.py -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q` (validate_kb, lint_skill, dedupe)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests/test_poll_feeds.py -q`

## QA Before Push
Cover injection in BOTH body and YAML fields; all 3 feed formats (OSV/ATLAS/Atom); the 3 gate verdict states. Use a temp dir so the real `evals/feed-state.json` / `gate-verdict.json` are never touched.

## Risks / Dependencies
The live "agent reads poisoned KB and still respects the gate" check needs a runtime — keep it an
**optional gated** sub-item; the deterministic gates/parsers are the load-bearing pass criterion. Don't
clobber real feed-state / verdict files (temp dir only).

## Notes / Open Questions
Deterministic vs live is called out per AC. Existing tests (`test_gate_kb_edit`, `test_poll_feeds`,
`test_lint_skill`, `test_validate_kb`) already cover the happy path; this ticket adds the **poisoned**
fixtures and records the adversarial verdict.

title: T-13.5: QA-4 security-policy verdict (present/absent/malicious + F1-F3)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Record a dated verdict that the **security-policy** subsystem treats SECURITY.md/security.txt as
**untrusted data**: absent→advisory (no severity/cvss), present→merged draft preserving maintainer
facts, malicious→injection flagged with **no body echo**, output confined to `PATCHES/`; and the F1–F3
hardenings + the scope-never-suppresses rule hold. Evidence → `docs/qa/20260607/qa-4-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `_shared/scripts/policy_detect.py` — facts-only extraction; **`read_capped(..., limit=_READ_LIMIT=256 KiB)` (line 70)** (F2 read cap); ReDoS-bounded email regex.
- `sec-policy/scripts/parse_policy.py` — gap report (booleans/enums/section names only); injection denylist returns a BOOLEAN, never echoes the matched span.
- `sec-policy/scripts/propose_policy.py` — **F1: `_safe_write` opens with `os.O_NOFOLLOW` (line 154)** → ELOOP refuse on symlink; PATCHES symlink + `..`/separator confinement guards; existing body preserved verbatim as a prefix.
- `sec-policy/scripts/hygiene_advisory.py` — **`is_factual` (line 44)** guards the advisory message (F3); advisory carries no `severity`/`cvss`/`owasp`.
- `sec-report/scripts/policy_scope.py` — **"NEVER drops a finding and NEVER changes/lowers severity" (line 8)**, `annotate_findings_with_scope` (line 20) adds only `out_of_scope_per_policy` (ADR-018).

## Verification Workflow
Investigate → build 3 SECURITY.md fixtures (absent / present-minimal / malicious-with-injection) → run the detect→parse→propose→advisory→scope chain + the F1/F2/F3 probes → record verdict. TDD if any new test is added.

## Steps
1. Baseline: run sec-policy / _shared / sec-report unit suites (must be green).
2. ABSENT fixture: `present=False`; `propose` action=`create` into `PATCHES/`; hygiene advisory fires (factual, no severity/cvss).
3. PRESENT fixture: `present=True`; `propose` action=`modify`; merged file starts with the original body verbatim; missing sections appended; maintainer email/versions/scope unchanged.
4. MALICIOUS fixture: `injection_suspected=True`; serialized gap-report JSON contains **zero** occurrences of the injected spans.
5. F1: symlinked output file AND symlinked PATCHES dir each → `ValueError` ("symlink"); real target byte-unchanged; nothing escapes.
6. F2: `detect_reporting_channel` on two 200K+ adversarial inputs each < 1.0s; `read_capped` enforces ≤256 KiB.
7. F3: `is_factual(advisory_message)` true; imperative variants false.
8. Scope: `annotate_findings_with_scope([HIGH], terms)` keeps severity+cvss, only adds the annotation.
9. Write `qa-4-verdict.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Absent→advisory (no severity/cvss) — probe: fixture run asserts `present=False`, advisory `kind=hygiene-advisory`, no `severity`/`cvss`.
- [ ] Present→modify, verbatim preservation — probe: merged text `.startswith(original)`; appended sections present; contact preserved.
- [ ] Malicious→flagged, no echo — probe: `injection_suspected=True`; `grep -c` injected spans in gap JSON → 0.
- [ ] F1 symlink write-escape refused (file + dir) — probe: both `propose()` calls raise `ValueError`; target unchanged; `pytest …/sec-policy/scripts/tests -k symlink -q` exit 0.
- [ ] F2 ReDoS bounded + read cap — probe: adversarial inputs < 1.0s; `read_capped` ≤262144 bytes; `pytest -k "redos or read_capped" -q` exit 0.
- [ ] F3 advisory factual — probe: `is_factual(msg)` True; imperatives False.
- [ ] Scope never suppresses a HIGH — probe: severity/cvss unchanged, only `out_of_scope_per_policy` added.
- [ ] Output confined to `PATCHES/` — probe: `propose` writes only under `PATCHES/`; `..`/separator names rejected.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-4-verdict.md` recorded.

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-policy/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`

## QA Before Push
Cover the 3 fixtures + both F1 symlink variants + F2 timing + scope HIGH. Clean `/tmp/*_qa4` fixtures.

## Risks / Dependencies
`O_NOFOLLOW` is best-effort on non-Unix; the `_assert_not_symlink` pre-check is the cross-platform guard — note the Windows caveat in the verdict. Prefer `uv run pytest` over ad-hoc `sys.path` scripts.

## Notes / Open Questions
security.txt (RFC 9116) covered by existing unit tests; the 3 fixtures focus on SECURITY.md. Hygiene message is intentionally frozen/factual (changing it is a code change).

- `packaging/validate_manifest.py` — CLI `python validate_manifest.py [repo]`, exit 0 valid / 1 invalid; rejects unknown top-level keys (T-12.7).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/init_profile.py` — writes `<repo>/.white-hacker/project-profile.json`; schema `project_profile_schema.json` (verified present) with `additionalProperties:false` (blocks any planted identity key); refuses to write non-factual values.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/sessionstart_project_facts.py` — **F-001 allowlist**: `_ALLOWED_TOKEN = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9 ._/+-]{1,64}$")` (**line 52**), `_MAX_WORDS` (line 53), separator-aware `_WORD_SEP_RE` (defeats packed imperatives). Registered via `hooks/hooks.json` (SessionStart).
- Identity carriage: **no `CLAUDE.md` anywhere under `plugins/white-hacker/`** (verified); posture lives in `agents/white-hacker.md` (kind=agent, tools scoped, model=opus).

## Verification Workflow (investigate → probe → record)
Existing suites already pin behavior (sec-init, hooks, packaging). This ticket *runs them as a flow* on
real + crafted-malicious fixtures and records the verdict. New malicious-profile fixture is throwaway (`/tmp`).

## Steps
1. Manifest: run validator + `claude plugin validate` on the repo.
2. init_profile: run the CLI on a fixture repo; jsonschema-validate the written profile; confirm the `security_policy` block (7 keys).
3. SessionStart F-001: feed a crafted profile (markdown role-framing + imperative + `attacker.com` in `frameworks`/`path`/`reporting_channel`) and confirm none of it appears in `additionalContext`.
4. Identity carriage: `find … -iname CLAUDE.md` empty; agent.md declares posture/tools/model.
5. Write `qa-3-verdict.md` (commands, outputs, exit codes, commit hash, PASS/FAIL).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest valid — probe: `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py . ; echo $?` → `0` + "OK"; `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` → ✔.
- [ ] init_profile writes a schema-valid profile carrying `security_policy` — probe: run CLI on a fixture; `jq .security_policy <profile>` shows all 7 keys; jsonschema validates.
- [ ] SessionStart drops a crafted malicious profile — probe: pipe the fixture event; `grep -c '# SYSTEM\|developer mode\|attacker.com\|ignore all instructions'` on the emitted context → `0`; context still `is_factual`.
- [ ] F-001 allowlist holds + legit tokens survive — probe: `pytest …/hooks/tests/test_sessionstart_facts.py -k "f001 or f3" -q` → exit 0 (out-of-vocab/markdown/packed dropped; `fastapi`/`lang-python`/`github-pvr` kept).
- [ ] Identity carriage — probe: `find plugins/white-hacker -iname CLAUDE.md` empty; `grep -E '^(name|kind|tools|model):' plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` all present.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-3-verdict.md` recorded (PASS/FAIL + commit hash).

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project packaging --with pytest pytest packaging/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest …/tests/test_sessionstart_facts.py -q`
- `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## QA Before Push
Malicious-profile fixture exercises markdown / OOV-imperative / packed-separator / per-field cases; confirm legit single-token values still render (no over-redaction). Clean `/tmp` fixtures.

## Risks / Dependencies
`O_NOFOLLOW`/path specifics are F-001-adjacent only here. Needs `uv` + `claude` CLI on PATH (subscription; no API key). Capture `git rev-parse HEAD` for the verdict.

## Notes / Open Questions
The two closed-vocabulary fields (`security_policy.path`, `reporting_channel`) use exact-set matching (stricter than the shape allowlist) — verify both render only canonical values.

title: Maintenance/staleness health: cadence/EOL gate on ADMIT + a staleness signal in the MONITOR feed
description:
## Goal
Add the MAINTENANCE/STALENESS health dimension to the tool lifecycle — the trivy-mcp lesson (an
unmaintained wrapper pinning a stale binary is a supply-chain risk distinct from compromise). Two parts:
(1) ADMIT gate — a tool must be actively maintained (release cadence, last-commit recency, NOT
EOL/feature-complete/archived); extends wh-xn0's admissibility gates + adds a registry health column.
(2) MONITOR signal — the sec-kb-refresh feed polls staleness (latest-release age, archived/EOL/
feature-complete flags via the GitHub API) → proposes a human-gated swap BEFORE a tool is a liability
(would have caught gitleaks→Betterleaks and trivy-mcp).

## Notes
Deterministic where possible (a maintenance score from release/commit dates — Policy 5, not LLM judgment).
Human-gated proposals only (ADR-012, never auto-swap). Reconcile with wh-xn0 (ADMIT gates) + wh-5es (the
feed) + the strategy (docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tool-registry gains a maintenance/health field; ADMIT rejects EOL/archived/feature-complete tools (or admits with a flagged caveat + a tracked successor, e.g. gitleaks→Betterleaks)
- [ ] sec-kb-refresh polls a staleness signal (release age / archive flag) and proposes a human-gated registry note when a tool crosses a staleness threshold
- [ ] deterministic maintenance check (release/commit dates), not an LLM judgment
- [ ] reconciled with wh-xn0 (gates) + wh-5es (feed); never auto-swaps

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (ADMIT + MONITOR stages); wh-xn0, wh-5es; ADR-012.

title: Spike: GDPR PII data-minimization in the review pipeline (findings/TRIAGE/KB/report) (→ next-available ADR)
description:
## Research Question

The agent reads **untrusted code that may contain personal data** (PII in source, comments, fixtures,
logs, seed/test data). GDPR data-minimization asks: **what personal data may end up in the agent's OWN
artifacts, and what rule/redaction keeps it out?** This is a POSTURE decision on the agent's pipeline
output — disjoint from the *tool* admissibility gates (**Spike A → ADR-024**). Each RQ → a decision:

- **RQ1 — Enumerate the PII leak surface.** Which output fields can carry copied source / personal
  data: `VULN-FINDINGS.json` (`exploit_scenario`, `first_link` snippets), `TRIAGE.json`,
  `ai-attack-kb/reference/*` entries, `SECURITY-REPORT.md`? Map each to its risk.
- **RQ2 — The rule (extend, don't duplicate; don't mis-state).** The posture already says "**never
  store credentials in output, logs, tickets, or KB**" (`agents/white-hacker.md:41`) and the triage
  decision-maker already sees only the **minimal** `{file,line,category,diff}` (`:69`). PII ⊋
  credentials, so this is a genuine **extension of :41** (credentials → all personal data). Decide the
  precise rule. NOTE: do **NOT** frame it as "extend `{file,line,category,diff}`" — that tuple is the
  minimal contract *by design* and excludes prose; the question is whether the **snippet/diff/
  exploit_scenario** fields need a **redaction/constraint** so PII isn't copied verbatim into an
  artifact (make it leak *less*, not carry *more*).
- **RQ3 — Enforcement: deterministic or posture-only?** Is a deterministic redaction pass warranted
  (reuse the `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` pattern — it already never emits the secret value), or
  is a posture rule + the minimal-context contract sufficient? If deterministic, where does it run
  (report render? finding emit?) and does it need a schema constraint?
- **RQ4 — Data-residency / retention.** `SECURITY-REPORT.md` is gitignored (transient) — good; the
  **KB is git-tracked → must carry ZERO PII** (already partly covered by ADR-012 "never store the
  security contact in the KB"). State the residency + no-retention posture for each artifact.
- **RQ5 — (OUT OF SCOPE — DOCUMENT ONLY) the agent's own model call.** Reviewed code → Anthropic for
  the LLM pass is a GDPR **processor** consideration: it runs on the user's Claude Code subscription
  under the user's lawful basis. **Document the data-flow** (what leaves the machine, the processor
  relationship) in the report; do **NOT** attempt to solve it here (it is inherent to the model).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1 day (a posture decision + a small enforcement call; lighter than Spike A).

## Background / Context

- GDPR **data-minimization** + the **Agents Rule of Two** (`agents/white-hacker.md:39-41`: never
  co-locate untrusted input + secrets + egress). The existing posture covers **credentials**; PII is
  the strict superset this spike generalizes to.
- **Sibling of Spike A** (tool admissibility / ADR-024). This one touches the agent's OWN output
  (agent md posture + `sec-report` render + finding schema), **NOT** `tool-registry.md` — disjoint
  files, so the two can run in parallel (Policy 3).
- **Binding to cite, not re-debate:** ADR-008 (recall/precision separation — the minimal triage
  contract), ADR-012 (KB never stores the security contact — the PII precedent for the KB),
  the agent Posture (`:41`), the existing `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` (the redaction pattern).

## Investigation Approach

1. **Leak-surface map (RQ1).** Read `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json` + the agent's output
   contract; list every field that can hold a copied source snippet or free-text that could embed PII.
2. **Extend-vs-redact (RQ2/RQ3).** Compare: (a) posture rule only ("never copy PII verbatim; reference
   by `file:line`, not value"), vs (b) a deterministic redaction pass modeled on
   `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` (which proves the never-emit-the-value pattern works + is tested).
   Pick the lightest sufficient enforcement (Policy 2).
3. **KB residency (RQ4).** Confirm the KB-no-PII rule (extends ADR-012) + the transient/gitignored
   status of the live report; state the retention posture per artifact.
4. **Model-call note (RQ5).** Draft a short, accurate GDPR-processor data-flow note (document only).

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
1. Read the finding schema + agent output contract + secrets-scan/redact.py before deciding.
2. Web search (2026) only if needed for GDPR data-minimization best-practice phrasing.
3. Decide the lightest sufficient rule (posture vs deterministic redaction) — Policy 2.
4. Follow-up: ONE implementation ticket.
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_gdpr_pii_data_minimization.md`.
- [ ] **ADR-025 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** (append-only): the PII data-minimization rule (extends
      `:41` credentials→PII), the enforcement decision (posture vs deterministic redaction), the
      KB/report residency + no-retention posture, and the documented model-call GDPR-processor note.
- [ ] ONE implementation follow-up ticket (the posture line in the agent md + any redaction helper/
      schema constraint) — no production code in this spike.

### Research Report File
```bash
touch docs/research/20260609_gdpr_pii_data_minimization.md
```

## Findings Template

```markdown
# Research: GDPR PII data-minimization in the review pipeline
**Date:** 2026-06-09  **Spike Ticket:** <id>  **Status:** Draft | Final
## Summary
## PII leak surface (per output artifact/field)
## The rule (extend :41 credentials→PII) + why NOT "extend {file,line,category,diff}"
## Enforcement: posture-only vs deterministic redaction (redact.py pattern)
## Data-residency / retention (KB git-tracked = ZERO PII; report gitignored/transient)
## Model-call GDPR-processor note (documented, not solved)
## Recommendation → ADR-025 + ONE impl ticket
## References
```

## Create Follow-up Tickets — EXIT CRITERIA

1. **ADR-025 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** — the PII data-minimization rule + enforcement + residency +
   the model-call note.
2. **THEN exactly ONE implementation ticket:** the posture line in `agents/white-hacker.md` (extend
   `:41` to PII), the chosen enforcement (a redaction pass or a schema/contract constraint), and tests
   that PII placed in a fixture finding does NOT appear verbatim in the emitted artifact.

- [ ] ADR-025 appended
- [ ] One implementation ticket created

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1–RQ4 answered → a stated decision; RQ5 documented as out-of-scope (model-call note).
- [ ] The rule is framed as an **extension of `:41` (credentials→PII)** + a redaction/constraint —
      NOT as "extend `{file,line,category,diff}`".
- [ ] Enforcement decision justified by Policy 2 (lightest sufficient).
- [ ] KB-no-PII + report-residency posture stated.
- [ ] ADR-025 appended; ONE impl ticket filed; no production code changed in this spike.
- [ ] Research report written; timebox (~1 day) respected.

## Notes / Open Questions

- Likely outcome: a **posture rule** ("reference PII by `file:line`, never copy the value") + reuse the
  proven `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` pattern for the snippet/report path — rather than a new
  schema field. Confirm against the leak-surface map.
- The KB rule (no PII) extends ADR-012's "never store the security contact" — make the generalization
  explicit, append-only.
- Sibling: **Spike A (ADR-024)** owns tool license/data-egress admissibility; this ticket is disjoint.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09: (1) DE-HARD-CODE ADR-025 → next-available at write time (wh-xn0 also pre-claims 024; both OPEN = race; first-to-write takes 024). (2) MISCITE: the 'never store the security contact in the KB' precedent is ADR-018 (docs/ARD.md:239), NOT ADR-012 (only the KB-vs-checklist structure) — re-point RQ4/Background/Notes to ADR-018. (3) the body assumes 024-is-taken but bd shows no dep on wh-xn0 — declare the coupling or drop the specific-number cross-refs. Posture (:41, :69) + redact.py reuse verified CORRECT.

title: Refine installation: pinned + verified remote installer (curl|bash run in target)
description:
## Goal
A remote ONE-COMMAND installer (curl|bash) run INSIDE a target project that PINS a stable version tag,
shallow-clones to a temp dir, VERIFIES integrity, and vendors/installs white-hacker — and is itself an
exemplar of the supply-chain hygiene the agent preaches (ADR-006 pin-and-verify; 'the agent that scans
supply chains must not be a supply-chain victim').

## Prerequisites
- A SIGNED stable git tag exists to pin (plugin.json is v0.1.0 but no git tag yet — cut + sign one).
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json + plugins/white-hacker/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (present, validates).
- git + (for the plugin lane) the 'claude' CLI.

## Steps
1. DECIDE THE MODEL (step 1; append an ADR to docs/ARD.md if the distribution model changes):
   (a) PLUGIN lane (ADR-017) — remote wrapper around 'claude plugin marketplace add jaigouk/white-hacker'
       + install, pinned to a tag; auto-updates; per-user.
   (b) VENDORED lane — temp-clone at tag -> copy payload into the target .claude/{agents,skills,commands,
       hooks} or a .white-hacker/ dir; self-contained/committed in the target (good for CI / no per-user
       install) BUT FORKS the skills (drift) and contradicts ADR-017's 'ship generic base + project
       companion'. If chosen, document the refresh path (re-run at a new tag).
   (c) BOTH lanes (plugin for shared use; vendored for committed/CI). Pick explicitly — do not silently fork.
2. Cut + GPG-sign a stable tag (e.g. v0.1.0); installer pins it; verify with 'git verify-tag'.
3. Build the installer (best practice, dogfooding ADR-006):
   - 'git clone --branch <TAG> --depth 1 <repo> "$(mktemp -d)"' + 'trap ... EXIT' cleanup;
   - INTEGRITY VERIFY: signed tag (git verify-tag) and/or a pinned SHA and/or a SHA-256 of the payload —
     the installer must fetch NOTHING unpinned;
   - IDEMPOTENT vendoring: back up / refuse-to-clobber an existing .claude/.white-hacker payload;
   - curl|bash SAFETY: FUNCTION-WRAPPER (whole body in a fn called on the LAST line so a truncated
     download defines functions but runs nothing) + 'set -euo pipefail' + prereq checks +
     '--dry-run'/'--unattended' flags + a review-before-run note;
   - then run sec-init -> writes <target>/.white-hacker/project-profile.json.
4. Update README 'Install & onboarding' with the remote one-liner + the security/pin-verify note.
5. AUDIT the installer itself (run the white-hacker review on it; assert no unpinned fetch).

## Files to Create/Modify
- install.sh (extend/supersede the just-added local one) OR a new install/ entrypoint + a hosted raw URL.
- README.md (Install & onboarding section).
- docs/ARD.md — an ADR IF the distribution model changes (step 1).
- packaging/ — N/A unless a packaged artifact/checksum is added.

## Verification
- 'bash -n install.sh' + shellcheck clean.
- A '--dry-run' in a scratch target prints the pinned clone + verify + idempotent copy WITHOUT mutating.
- Re-run is idempotent (backs up, never silently clobbers).
- grep the script: the ONLY fetched ref is the pinned tag/SHA — zero unpinned 'git clone <branch-less>'/curl of an unpinned URL.

## Rollback
Additive (install.sh + README + optional ADR) — revert the commit. Vendored files in a target are
user-owned; the installer backs up before overwrite, so no destructive change.
design: Dogfood narrative: white-hacker is a SECURITY tool, so its installer must EXEMPLIFY ADR-006 pin-and-verify + curl|bash best practices (function-wrapper for partial-download safety, signed-tag/SHA-256 verify, temp-clone+trap-cleanup, idempotent backup). Patterns: fzf (shallow/temp clone + markered edits), oh-my-zsh (--unattended + backup), rustup/starship (pinned release + checksum). title: Category-alias-aware eval scorer (AI/auth/crypto classes)
description: The opus re-baseline (wh-aj6) + spike-11 both show the eval gate's WEAK point is score.py's exact category match, not agent recall. 8 of opus's 10 'FN' were the vuln FOUND at the exact line but labeled with a defensible-but-different vocab category (LLM05 improper-output-handling vs LLM06 excessive-agency; JWT crypto vs AuthN/AuthZ; secret crypto vs config; prompt-injection vs rag-poisoning). Detection-recall is 116/118=0.983 vs strict J=0.917. WHAT: add a category-alias map (or a --lenient mode) so defensible cross-class equivalences don't read as misses; OR calibrate the label vocab for the AI classes. This is spike-11's 'the lever is the scorer, not the corpus'. Do NOT silently inflate the score (that's what produced the prior fake J=1.0) — make the leniency explicit + audited. files: evals/score.py + the label vocab.
acceptance_criteria: score.py supports an explicit, audited category-alias/lenient mode; the 8 wh-aj6 disagreements no longer read as misses under it; strict mode unchanged; documented
close_reason: Opt-in alias-aware lenient scorer in score.py (4 audited symmetric aliases). Strict default UNCHANGED (gate=0.917, drift-guard green); lenient lens=0.975 recovers 7/8 defensible disagreements. py-llm05-ssrf un-aliased (too broad); no silent inflation. 14 score + 183 evals green.

title: Hardened Docker sandbox for deps-scan/S8 testing against untrusted manifests + the OSSF malware snapshot
description: WHY: the deps-scan supply-chain floor is the component that ingests UNTRUSTED package manifests + the real ossf/malicious-packages snapshot (wh-8qw). The floor is proven inert (pure stdlib JSON+regex, no install/exec/network), and the S8 smoke test (wh-2v5) is synthetic+offline — but cloning the real OSSF repo and especially testing against live-malware datasets (DataDog/pypi_malregistry) should NEVER touch the host. Agents Rule of Two: isolate untrusted input from egress. WHAT: a dedicated, opt-in Docker sandbox (Dockerfile + run.sh + README + .dockerignore) that runs the deps-scan tests + the floor with --network none, --read-only, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root UID, no host writes (read-only mounts only). Base image PINNED by digest (ADR-006). Documents the wh-8qw real-snapshot flow: clone+pin in an isolated network-on step into a volume; analyze that volume network-OFF. The native offline workflow is unchanged (uv run pytest still works, zero host risk). NOTE: this de-risks but does not replace the native test.
acceptance_criteria: docker/ sandbox with Dockerfile (non-root, pinned base, copies only the floor+_shared+sec-detect), run.sh (build|test|scan with the full hardening flags), README (threat model + usage + wh-8qw snapshot flow), .dockerignore; shell scripts pass bash -n; adversarial hardening review (escape/egress/host-access) passes; native uv-run deps-scan tests still green (78)
close_reason: Hardened docker/deps-scan-sandbox/ shipped; 5-lens adversarial review (analysis lane sealed; blocker schema-COPY fixed; fetch container hardened); default test lane verified green (67 passed) via faithful Py3.13 layout sim. docker not built here (no daemon).

title: T-12.9b: Restore eval headroom — multi-file / real-CVE-in-context corpus
description:
## Goal
The 115-case corpus is SATURATED (J=1.0; the current agent aces single-file pairs — see t-12.9-report.md
+ the ablation showing only a weakened agent drops to 0.8). Add cases that need:
- cross-file taint (source and sink in DIFFERENT files -> the reviewer must build a call graph),
- real un-patched CVE diffs in surrounding noise (distractors, business logic; not minimal snippets),
- genuinely-ambiguous severity/category (test triage, not just detection).
So the CURRENT agent scores < 1.0 (real measurable headroom). May need a multi-file case shape +
score.py file-set matching.

- [ ] >= 3 multi-file / CVE-in-context cases the current agent does NOT trivially ace (J < 1.0 on the subset), neutralized filenames

Carry from Phase-12 T-12.9. Pairs with T-12.5 (opus re-baseline).

title: Fix cwd-fragile manifest test (test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys uses relative '.')
description:
## Goal / Problem
`packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py:361` (test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys)
calls `vm.validate_marketplace(".")` with a RELATIVE path — the ONLY real-repo test not using the
module-level `REPO_ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]` (line 25). It passes from repo
root, FAILS from any other cwd (reproduced from /tmp: 1 failed, 39 passed). CI runs from root so it is
green there, but the test is cwd-fragile. CONFIRMED real (wh-4ym.4 verify + grooming 2026-06-08).

## Workflow (TDD)
- RED: `cd /tmp && uv run --project <repo>/packaging --with pytest pytest <repo>/packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py::test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys -q` -> FAIL.
- GREEN: change `vm.validate_marketplace(".")` -> `vm.validate_marketplace(REPO_ROOT)` (match siblings).
- Re-run from /tmp AND repo root -> both pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] line 361 uses REPO_ROOT, no literal "." — probe: `grep -n 'validate_marketplace(".")' packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py` returns nothing.
- [ ] packaging suite passes from ANY cwd — probe: `cd /tmp && uv run --project <repo>/packaging --with pytest pytest <repo>/packaging/tests -q` -> exit 0, 40 passed.

## File Ownership
`packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py` (line 361) — single file.

## Quality Gate
`uv run --project packaging --with pytest pytest packaging/tests -q` (40 passed, from any cwd).

Ready — no deps. Found by wh-4ym.4 (QA-3) verify.

title: Fix poll_feeds ElementTree DeprecationWarning (line 61) + YAML-injection regression test
description:
## Goal / Problem
Two items from wh-4ym.6 (QA-7) verify; grooming 2026-06-08 refined the scope:
1. **REAL — ElementTree truth-value DeprecationWarning** at `poll_feeds.py:61`
   (`idc = kids.get("id") or kids.get("guid")` — `or` on an Element). Under
   `-W error::DeprecationWarning`, test_parse_atom FAILS (1 failed, 7 passed). Fix to explicit `is not None`.
2. **YAML injection — ALREADY SAFE (no rewrite)**: `render_entry` (poll_feeds.py:105) uses
   `yaml.safe_dump(entry, ...)`, so feed text -> dict -> safe_dump is escaped; a crafted feed cannot
   inject YAML keys. Scope reduced to ADD a regression test that PINS this (cheap guard).

## Workflow (TDD)
- RED: `pytest -W error::DeprecationWarning .../test_poll_feeds.py -q` -> test_parse_atom FAILS.
- GREEN: `poll_feeds.py:61` -> `idc = kids.get("id"); idc = idc if idc is not None else kids.get("guid")`.
  Add a poisoned-feed test: a feed item whose title/summary contains `: `, newlines, and `injected_key: x`
  -> `render_entry` output `yaml.safe_load`s to a dict whose top-level keys == the intended schema keys
  (no feed-injected key).
- REFACTOR: keep minimal; confirm line 59 (`link is not None and link.get(...)`) is already safe.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] no DeprecationWarning — probe: `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest .../tests/test_poll_feeds.py -q -W error::DeprecationWarning` exit 0.
- [ ] poisoned-feed YAML regression test — probe: new test asserts no feed-injected top-level YAML key in render_entry output; exit 0.
- [ ] sec-kb-refresh suite green, 0 warnings.

## File Ownership
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py` (line 61) + `tests/test_poll_feeds.py`.

Ready — no deps. Found by wh-4ym.6 (QA-7) verify. The original "harden YAML rendering" framing is
RESOLVED-as-already-safe (safe_dump); the load-bearing fix is the DeprecationWarning.
close_reason: Closed

title: Real-CVE-in-noise eval corpus for headroom (research, uncertain)
description:
## Goal
T-12.9 (single-file) + T-12.9b (cross-file) proved synthetic paired cases are ACED by the frontier
agent (J=1.0). The only realistic lever to restore measurable headroom: real un-patched CVE diffs in
real business-logic noise / many-file indirection / obfuscation.
## DEFERRED / RESEARCH
Research-scale + uncertain (the agent may recognize known CVEs). Pairs with wh-aj6 (opus re-baseline).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >= 3 real-CVE-in-noise cases where the CURRENT agent scores < 1.0 (neutralized; baseline + drift-guard updated)
      OR a documented spike concluding synthetic+real headroom is infeasible against the frontier agent.
See docs/qa/20260607/t-12.9b-report.md for the finding that motivates this.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-08 VERDICT=RESEARCH/DEFER. Corpus is saturated (J=1.0; Phase-13 found synthetic headroom infeasible) → real-CVE-in-noise value is UNCERTAIN (likely more anchors, not headroom). Same drift-guard coupling as wh-qyf (lands atomically with a re-baseline). Keep deferred (P4); if pursued, pair with wh-aj6. files: evals/corpus/ + baseline.
close_reason: Path B: spike-11 (docs/research/spike-real-cve-headroom-2026-06.md) concludes real-CVE headroom infeasible under the current scorer (presence-not-rank); the lever is the scorer, not the corpus. Unblocks wh-aj6.

value: Dev's Docker runtime = Rancher Desktop (moby/dockerd engine), chosen 2026-06 over OrbStack (commercial license needed) and Podman. docker CLI at ~/.rd/bin/docker (on PATH via .zshrc/.bashrc); active context = rancher-desktop -> unix://~/.rd/docker.sock. NOTE: /var/run/docker.sock does NOT exist (admin socket access not granted) — the deps-scan sandbox uses the docker CLI/context so it's fine, but tools expecting the default socket need Rancher Prefs -> Administrative Access. First-run was slow because Kubernetes is still enabled (Prefs -> Kubernetes to disable for faster/lighter Docker-only). Sandbox verified: docker build + run.sh test -> 67 passed, 1 deselected (docker 29.1.3).

Floor-scored eval cases (supply-chain, S1-S8) differ structurally from agent-scored cases: (1) scored by deterministic supply_chain.scan, NOT the LLM agent — an agent run marks them FN and depresses J, so their findings_snapshot entries must come from the floor; (2) need PER-VARIANT PROJECT SUBDIRS (vulnerable_variant/ + benign_lookalike/ each with package.json+lockfile) because the floor scans a DIRECTORY, not a flat file; (3) promote_finding.py builds only the flat 4-file case, so it CANNOT promote subdir cases — human hand-places or extends it; (4) floor findings are manifest-level (line 0) so the label uses line=1 as a tol=3 placeholder. Worked example + reproduce: PATCHES/wh-qyf/ (wh-qyf proposal).

When a design-ticket SPIKE's grounding/researcher CONFIRMS load-bearing threat-intel (a real CVE/GHSA, e.g. the TeamPCP/Trivy compromise), MATERIALIZE the verification (RQ1) + exposure (RQ2) to docs/research/<date>_<topic>.md AT GROOMING — do not leave confirmed, sourced findings only in the ticket body + the gitignored .claude/agent-memory/. The full design can still be the spike's deliverable; but verified threat-intel belongs in docs/research/ immediately (repo convention: research .md under docs/). Example: docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md (wh-562), 20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md (wh-5es).
